title: Accounting Professionalism
description: Introduction to the accounting profession, career options, professional responsibilities, and codes of conduct. Professionalism, networking, interviewing, job search, and technical writing skills are developed.

title: Intro Financial Accounting(CT)
description: Introduction to financial accounting principles and procedures, including transaction analysis, journalizing, financial statement preparation, business formations, internal control, and financial statement analysis.  This course meets Core 1/Critical Thinking requirement.

title: Intro Managerial Accounting
description: Introduction to managerial accounting for use in managerial control and decision making.  the course explores cost application methods, budgeting, profitability analysis, and decision-making models.

title: Acct for Entrepreneurs
description:
Principles, concepts and problems of accounting relevant to decision making for entrepreneurs
including: pro forma financial statements, cash flow, securing financing and cost structures.
NOT OPEN TO STUDENTS IN THE LEWIS COLLEGE OF BUSINESS

title: Intermediate Accounting I
description: Study of U.S. and International accounting standards related primarily to assets and financial statement preparation.  Accounting standards research and application of Excel are integrated throughout the course.

title: Intermediate Accounting II
description: Study of U.S. and International accounting standards related primarily to liabilities and revenue recognition.  Accounting standards research and application of Excel are integrated throughout the course.

title: Cost Accounting I
description: A study of fundamental cost accounting concepts and objectives including project cost accumulation, cost-volume-profit analysis, direct costing, budget techniques, standard costing, and differential cost analysis.

title: Acc Information Systems
description: Introduction to accounting systems. Emphasis on concepts of analysis, design, and implementation of accounting systems with attention on internal and audit trail.

title: Federal Taxation
description: Problems and procedures of income tax accounting.

title: Forensic & Investigative Acct
description: This course introduces students to forensic accounting focusing on criminal litigation and investigations of fraud.

title: Governmental Accounting
description: A study of the use of accounting information in the financial management of governmental and non-profit entities.

title: Adv Accounting Problems
description: Selected problems in advanced accounting principles and procedures.

title: Managerial Accounting
description: The managerial approach to budgetary control.

title: Auditing I
description: A study of the theory and procedures of auditing and the legal and social responsibilities of the auditor.

title: Internal Auditing
description: This course discusses the applicable current internal auditing theory and procedures, including a review of corporate governance and risk assessment.

title: Accounting Analytics
description: This course studies the impact of data analytics on accounting and auditing and explores when and how various analytical procedures and tools can be used to address business questions.

title: Federal Income Tax II
description: Advanced course in taxation with emphasis on corporations, partnerships, estates, trusts, gifts, valuation and liquidity problems, and tax administration and practice.

title: Special Topics
description: Study of an advanced topic not normally covered in other courses.  Accounting majors only, with permission of Department Chair.

title: Internship
description: A supervised internship in which the student works for a business firm/agency to gain practical experience in the student's major. The program of work and study will be defined in advance and the student's performance will be evaluated.

title: Professional and Ethics Sem
description: Designed to increase the student's awareness of the accounting environment, emphasizing ethics.  Communication skills are improved through impromptu speaking, written reports, group projects and formal presentations.  Capstone Course.

title: Intro to Career & Technical Ed
description: Designed to provide the new teacher guidance and supervision in implementing strategies in instructional planning, student engagement, and classroom management. Incorporates reflection and basic assessment strategies for students and instruction.

title: Occupational Analysis
description: This course assists the CTC instructor in analyzing, classifying, and describing occupations, analysis procedures for determining CTC education curriculum, course content, technical skills, and knowledge.

title: Course Const & Planning in CTE
description: Analysis of procedures for determining appropriate CTE curriculum content, program goals and objectives. Involvement of advisory committees for CTE. Explore factors, principles, and techniques om developing a CTE course.

title: Safety in CTE
description: Establishment of the teacher role, liability and accountability in providing a safe learning/working environment for CTE students.  Study of best-practice approaches to accident prevention. Introduction to laws and regulatory agencies.

title: Methods of Examination in CTE
description: This course prepares the CTE instructor to develop oral, written and performance evaluation instruments. The CTE instructor will develop and use progress monitoring strategies, feedback tools, and determine grading procedures.

title: Coordination of Cooperative Ed
description: Methods and techniques for evaluating and selecting work stations, student selection, placement and follow-up.  The WVDE Experiential Learning Guide is used for placement of students in structured work-based experiences.

title: Computer Applications in CTE
description: Introduction to computer-related terminology, internet concepts and types of software to be used by the CTE teacher. Assignments/Activities provide CTE teachers with assistance on using computer/software to enhance classroom productivity.

title: History and Philosophy of CTE
description: Historical influences in the development of Career and Technical education in America and Europe; motivating influences and the implications of philosophy in modern Career and Technical education.

title: Coord CTE Youth Activities
description: Course is designed to help the teacher of a career technical or health education program to organize and develop a student organization that is co-curricular to their particular program area.

title: Teach Special Students in CTE
description: A teacher and program approach to analyzing procedures for identifying CTE curriculum, support, instructional planning, student assessment in teaching students with special learning challenges and needs in career preparation programs.

title: Occup Update Back to Industry
description: An occupational specialization updating experience for the in-service, career technical teacher.  In-service teacher will gain new knowledge and skill in an occupational specialization through back-to-industry experience.

title: Occup Update Bus./Ind. Enrich
description: Seminars or workshops designed for specific occupational specializations updating.  Special topics designed to provide the in-service, CTE teacher with new knowledge and skill required of workers in business and industry.

title: Occup Update Instructor Dev
description: Special seminars or workshops designed for specific instruction strategies and teaching techniques updating.  Special topics shall be designed that are required of teachers in the classroom/lab/clinical/co-op setting.

title: Occup Update Other
description: Seminars, workshops, or other educational opportunities/events designed for increasing capacity and skill level for the CTE educator.  Special topics will recognize best-practices in the classroom, school, and extended learning community.

title: Teaching Methods in CTE
description: Addresses shop/lab/classroom and individual/group instruction using multiple best practice strategies. The four modules in teaching in CTE is emphasized. Physical, social and academic factors relating to shop/lab/classroom techniques are addressed.

title: Senior Project in CTE
description: Student design and complete an interdisciplinary project as evidence of mastery of instructional planning and strategies, student assessment and classroom management skills in an individualized classroom or laboratory learning environment.

title: Cultural Anthropology (CT)
description: Introduction to the scientific study of culture with emphasis on the cultures of small-scale societies. This class emphasizes critical thinking skills.

title: Cultural Anthropol Honors (CT)
description: Introduction to the scientific study of culture with emphasis on the cultures of small-scale societies. This class emphasizes critical thinking skills. For the honors student.

title: Social Statistics
description: Introduction to statistical analysis of social data.

title: Archaeology
description: Introduction to the methods and theory of archaeology.

title: Archaeolog Field Train
description: Supervised instruction in on-site archaeological data collection, survey and excavation techniques.

title: Archaeological Analysis
description: Supervised instruction in processing and analyzing archaeological materials recovered by fieldwork.

title: World Prehistory
description: An introduction to the archaeology of pre-literate cultures, from the emergence of Homo Sapiens to the present.

title: Classical Archaeology
description: Archaeology of ancient Greece and Rome, and their colonies and imperial domains.

title: Biological Anthropology
description: This course is the scientific study of humans and their closest extinct and extant relatives from evolutionary, biological, behavioral, and biocultural perspectives.

title: Forensic Anthropology
description: Forensic Anthropology is the study of human remains in medicolegal contexts. This course teaches students scene processing methods, analyzing human remains, estimating time since death, and determining manner of death.

title: Ethnographic Research
description: A project-based introduction to ethnographic research design and practice.

title: Health, Culture & Society
description: A cross-cultural, historical, and bio-cultural examination through case-study of social and environmental factors that affect human health and disease.

title: US Culture and the Family
description: An historically and ethnographically informed consideration of the changing meaning and place of family and work in everyday American life, media, and politics.

title: Expressive Culture
description: Exploration of expressive cultural forms in the construction of personal and communal identities and their meaningful attachment to particular geographic places.

title: Anthropology through Film
description: Exploration of film as general anthropological field of interest with emphasis on understanding selected films as cultural texts and association with particular and changing cultural, political, economics and historical contexts.

title: Linguistic Anthropology
description: Introduction to the theories and methodologies of linguistic anthropology and to language as a cultural phenomenon and form of diversity.

title: Methods in Language & Culture
description: In this course, students will learn a variety of methods for studying language and culture used in anthropology, sociolinguistics, and applied linguistics by collecting and analyzing actual linguistic data

title: Principles of GIS
description: Introduction to Geographic Information Systems (GIS) principles, techniques, and applications for the social and natural sciences with emphasis on foundational geographic principles in a lecture/lab format.

title: Deconstructing Appalachia
description: Exploration of the historical and cultural significance of Appalachia in the American experience and imagination.

title: Appalachian Field Exp I
description: Supervised fieldwork in an Appalachian community studying the social and cultural characteristics of the area.

title: Arch Theory and Analysis
description: An introduction to archaeological theory and its application to the material record of cultures, past and present.

title: African Cultures
description: Comparative analysis of the ethnic groups of Africa, using archaeological and ethnographic data.

title: Oceania
description: Comparative analysis of the indigenous peoples and cultures of Melanesia and Polynesia, using archaeological and ethnographic data.

title: The Native Americans
description: Comparative analysis of the indigenous inhabitants of North America, using archaeological and ethnographic data. PR: Six hours of anthropology or departmental permission.

title: American Ethnicities
description: Comparative overview of historical and contemporary patterns of immigration, settlement, and inter-ethnic relations in the United States.

title: Crime and Custom
description: This course examines law and legal processes in diverse cultures from anthropological and law and society perspectives.

title: Design, Planning & Health
description: Social scientific study of how places where people live, work, and recreate affect quality of life and overall health with an emphasis on design decisions within urban and regional planning.

title: Disaster, Culture & Health
description: Study of contemporary environmental and social problems emphasizing health impacts of natural and technological disasters on communities around the world.

title: Culture and Environment
description: This course will examine the symbolic and structural dimensions of struggles over defining, organizing, and controlling the natural environment from biocultural perspective.

title: Culture in Ethnographies
description: In depth exploration and comparison of diverse cultural groups through reading and discussing ethnographic texts. PR: Six credit hours of anthropology or departmental permission.

title: National Identity
description: Exploration of cultural, political and economic processes that contribute to creation and maintenance of the modern national state as an imagined community.

title: Language, Gender and the Body
description: Uses methods and theories from anthropology, linguistics, and sociology to examine how gendered bodies in different cultures are constructed through ways of acting in the world.

title: Intro to Sociolinguistics
description: Sociolinguistics is the study of the effects of language in society relevant to discourse practices, language attitudes, variations, shifts and changes.

title: Special Topics
description: Study of topics of interest not covered in regularly scheduled courses.

title: Special Topics
description: Study of topics of interest not covered in regularly scheduled courses.

title: Internship
description: Supervised practicum founded on anthropological knowledge in a host institution. 40-45 hours of internship work correspond with 1 credit hour.

title: Anthropological Theory
description: Introduction to major theoretical traditions of anthropology with emphasis on the connection between fieldwork and development of theory.

title: Senior Seminar I
description: Application of anthropological theory and practice to individually designed projects. This course fulfills the capstone requirement for undergraduate anthropology majors.

title: Senior Seminar II
description: Application of anthropological theory and practice to individually designed projects.  Fulfills the capstone requirement for undergraduate majors.

description: The culminating recital for applied music study in the principal applied area. Must be taken with principal applied instrument course ( MUSA 380 - MUSA 399 )

title: Visual Culture and Research
description: Introduction to the functions of art applications of media, elements and principles of design, artists, and aesthetics. (Required for Art Majors)

title: Intro To Visual Art
description: An introduction to the methods and principles of the visual arts.  Students will consider the work of major artists in thematic contexts.  (For non-art majors only.)

title: Intro to Visual Art Honors
description: An introduction to the methods and principles of the visual arts.  Students will consider the work of major artists in thematic contexts.  (For non-art majors only.)

title: History of Art I (CT)
description: A survey of the history of art, emphasizing Europe, north Africa, and west Asia from the Bronze Age to the Middle Ages. This class emphasizes critical thinking skills.

title: History of Art II (CT)
description: A survey of the history of art from the Renaissance to the twentieth century. This class emphasizes critical thinking skills.

title: Japanese Art in History
description: This course will introduce Japanese architecture, art, and craft, highlighting the importance of understanding Japanese history and culture for appreciating its art.

title: Foundations: Grid/Chroma
description: Introduction to graphic design principles/software in one half-semester workshop and color theory/painting in another.  Students will develop visual, technical, and critical thinking skills by solving conceptual problems.

title: Foundations: Form/Space
description: Introduction to 3D physical form/space by exploring ceramics in one half-semester workshop and sculpture in another. Students will develop visual, technical, and critical thinking skills by solving conceptual problems.

title: Foundations: Record/Layer
description: Introduction to still life/figure drawing in one half-semester workshop and experimental drawing/mixed media in another.  Students will develop perceptual, technical, and creative problem solving skills.

title: Foundations: Surface/Matrix
description: Introduction to fiber art and textile design in one half-semester workshop and printmaking processes in another. Students will develop visual, technical, and critical thinking skills by solving conceptual problems.

title: Foundations: Frame/Time
description: Introduction to digital/film photography in one half-semester workshop and 4D design principles/video-based processes in another. Students will develop visual, technical, and critical thinking skills by solving conceptual problems.

title: Beginning Painting I
description: Basic techniques using color creatively based on an understanding of visual structural elements; various media including water, acrylic and oil based paints.

title: Special Topics
description: To be used for experimental courses. By permission only.

title: Special Topics
description: To be used for experimental courses. By permission only.

title: Special Topics
description: To be used for experimental courses. By permission only.

title: Special Topics
description: To be used for experimental courses. By permission only.

title: Foundations Review: BFA
description: Students must present art and design work from all foundations studio courses for faculty review.  Required for program advancement.  Art majors only.

title: Foundations Review: BA
description: Students present artwork from foundations courses for faculty review.  (Art Education 5-Adult:  218 not required).  Successful completion of ART 299 is a prerequisite for advanced studio courses.

title: Printmaking Processes
description: Experiments in the media of intaglio, lithography, serigraphy, relief collagraphs and new techniques in printmaking.

title: Relief Printmaking
description: Traditional and experimental approaches to relief printmaking, including woodcut, linocut, wood engraving, relief etching, Japanese techniques, monoprints, and other press and handprinting relief processes.

title: Surface Design
description: Introduction to surface design.  Student will learn various techniques to apply color on fabric surface.

title: Ceramics
description: Search for form and personal expressing through clay. Emphasis on handbuilding techniques, decorative processes and glaze application.

title: Sculpture
description: Emphasis on modeling in clay and exploring the potential of plaster, wood and other materials relevant to the area of sculpture.

title: Weaving
description: The student will demonstrate the ability to carry through the entire process for planning, through warping, threading, and weaving. Each will create unique art works while developing traditional technical skills.

title: Art Education: Elementary
description: An introduction to the materials and methods for teaching art in early childhood and elementary (PreK-6).  Stages of development, integrated curriculum design, assessment, and instructional strategies are emphasized.

title: Typography
description:
Practical studio skills: Typography, application of design principles in print and 
digital media, and introduction to the computer as a design tool.  Also, the designer's
relationship to clients and other professionals.

title: Installation Art With Fibers
description: The student will create installation art using a variety of fibers tools, materials, and processes with focus on self-expression and the exploration of structure, space, color, form, and meaning.

title: Graphic Design I
description: Sign combinations and visual structure, in relation to meaning of visual messages. Assignments include posters, advertising, information design, and corporate identity. Introduction to materials and procedures in the design process.

title: Intro to Photography
description: Introduction to black and white photography through basic techniques of camera controls, lighting, traditional wet lab, and digital imaging.

title: Graphic Design II
description: Applies the use of type and images to design for advertising, editorial, for instructional purposed. Involvement with extended design and layout problems.

title: Illustration
description: Conceptual and technical development of illustrations for editorial and advertising purposes.

title: Art/Design for Websites
description:
This course will focus on art and design considerations in creating web
sites.  Current software will be used to create graphics, video and audio
for web page and website design.

title: Wood Fundamentals: Furniture
description: The fundamental methods of wood furniture design and construction, including joinery techniques, will be taught.  Domestic and exotic woods will be used.

title: Silk Screen Printmaking
description: Experience with screen-printing stencil processes. The advanced student may also explore photographic stencilmaking and printing and a variety of surfaces.

title: Experimental Media Arts
description: Students explore experimental media through historical contexts, practices, and processes from the analog experiments of early 20th-century avant-garde cinema through contemporary digital practices in new media art.

title: Collagraphs
description: Printmaking using the collagraph plate or matrix, an additive method that employs both intaglio and relief techniques.

title: 35mm Slide Photography
description: Photographic composition, color, and creative pictorial vision along with camera controls are explored through making 35mm slides.

title: Image Visual: Darkroom
description: ART 324 is a continuation of material presented in ART 315 .  Students will explore black and white photography through a variety of cameras and techniques.

title: Image Visualization: Digital
description: ART 325 is an exploration using digital techniques.  Students will refine their personal vision using digital cameras, lighting and digital imaging software.

title: Filmmaking I
description: This hands-on introductory filmmaking course focuses on the fundamentals of cinematic storytelling through theory and practical techniques to develop a variety of short films.

title: Filmmaking II
description: Students work together to create short-form films learning advanced concepts of filmmaking. Working in various crew roles, students complete a short film project for public screening.

title: Cinematic Non-Fiction
description: Students will explore diverse styles, techniques, and storytelling approaches to create impactful and compelling, short-form non-fiction films.

title: 2D Animation
description: Students learn traditional and digital 2D animation through projects that explore conceptual development and formal principles, tools, and techniques.

title: Expanded Forms of Sculpture
description: Emphasizes alternative ways of creating sculpture.  We will investigate and create artworks guiding by historical examples of ephemeral art, performance, interventions, language, sound, social practice, and land art.

title: Intro to Digital Sculpture
description: Emphasis will be on the tools, materials and processes of additive and subtractive sculpture.  Both traditional techniques and digital technologies will be explored using materials like plastic, wood, and foam.

title: Welded Sculpture
description: A variety of techniques including oxygen/acetylene, arc and TIG welding will be studied and practiced in the process of direct metal sculpting.

title: Art Ed 2D-3D
description: Philosophy, media and methods for teaching art (emphasis for elementary level); includes laboratory experiences.

title: Art Education: Secondary
description: An introduction to the methods for teaching art at the secondary level (7-12). Authentic instruction and  classroom assessment strategies are emphasized.

title: Intro to Potter's Wheel
description: Basic throwing skills, surface enrichment and glaze application emphasized. Design analysis and production of functional form stressed.

title: Primitive Ceramic Tech
description: The study of local clay preparation and primitive firing and decorating techniques.

title: Problems in Porcelain
description: The formulation and use of porcelain in the production of utilitarian and sculptural form.

title: Watercolor Painting
description: Watercolor medium in expressing still life, landscape, and the human figure.

title: Advanced Watercolor
description: Advanced exploration of watercolor, inks and other fluid media. Emphasis will be on experimental methods and personal originality.

title: Painting III
description: Continues development for the intermediate level painter with emphasis on techniques and form, including varied supports, grounds, mediums such as encaustics, synthetic reins, egg tempera, acrylics and oils.

title: Mixed Media
description: Projects in painting, drawing and mixed media.

title: Mold Making & Casting
description: Advanced processes of piece and flexible mold making will be studied and practiced for the purpose of casting complex forms and limited edition sculpture.

title: 20th Century Art
description: A survey of major 20th Century artists works, styles, movements and media in a broad historical context with discussion of research methods and directed research project.

title: Professional Practice
description: Current views and practice: contemporary issues in art, safe practices in the studio, career opportunities, technology and art, and professional skills for artists.

title: Iconography of Mary
description: Traces the sources and evolution of Catholic doctrine and images of the Virgin Mary.

title: Figure Drawing
description: Practice in drawing from the posed human figure.

title: Tribal Arts
description: An introduction to the unique arts of so-called precivilized peoples with a two-fold emphasis: First, the European pre-historic; second, the non-European primitive.

title: Art & Arch of Ancient Egypt
description: History of the visual arts of ancient Egypt in the context of social and religious influences.

title: 19th Century Art
description: A survey of the development of architecture, painting, and sculpture in the Western World during the last century.

title: Art of Ancient Rome
description: Explores the art and architecture of ancient Rome in light of social and religious influences.

title: Art & Arch Italian Renaissance
description: Explores the art and architecture of the Italian Renaissance in the context of social and religious influences.

title: Northern Renaissance Art
description: Explores the art of Northern Europe during the Renaissance in light of social and religious influences.

title: Baroque Art
description: Analyzes Baroque art and the social milieu that influenced, commissioned, financed, and produced it.

title: Advanced Drawing
description: Drawing problems designed and executed by the individual student, in a variety of media, to develop unique imagery and increased technical skill.

title: Textile Construction
description: Explore various textile materials using ancient and contemporary processes and their applications in the development of 21st century crafts and products.

title: Textile Design
description: Textile design for possible commercial production, emphasizing the creation of numerous fabric samples and limited amounts of yardage.

title: Digital Process for Textiles
description: Students will use graphics software to produce original designs emphasizing the strength of these programs to quickly manipulate color palette, scale, and pattern to develop functional textiles for industrial use.

title: Photographic Lighting
description: Advanced course for students who have completely mastered the basics of photography.  Covers basic studio setup, creative use of the studio situation in portraits, still life and photo illustration.

title: Women and Art
description: Explores the relationship of women to art historically; as artists, as subject matter, and as patrons/consumers.

title: History of Photography
description: Selected survey of the history of photography investigating the prehistory and invention of photography, portraiture, landscape, social documentary, aesthetic, and experimental practice, post-modernism, and the digital age.

title: 19th Century Photo Processes
description: Explores traditional photographic processes that were prevalent during the 19th century combines with contemporary practices using digital techniques.  Emphasis on creative development and technical proficiency.

title: Photo Portfolio
description: Continued development of creative work with emphasis on preparation of portfolio and exhibition.

title: History of Medieval Art
description: Explores the art and architecture of the European Middle Ages in light of social and religious influences.

title: Byzantine and Islamic Art
description: Explores the art and architecture of the Byzantine Empire and neighboring medieval Islamic societies in light of social and religious influences.

title: Collective 37
description: Collective 37, Marshall University’s student-run communications agency, provides the opportunity to apply skills learned in other courses to complete work for clients. Course open to all majors. Permission only.

title: Illusion
description: Illusion explores the history and application of various illusionist effects to assist in the understanding of why artists incorporate illusion in their work and how these concepts convey relevant meaning.

title: Film Production Fundamentals
description: In this advanced workshop, students will work as small production teams with community organizations to create real-world client short film projects.

title: Video Art
description: This course explores video as an artistic medium, examining its historical roots in the 1960s and its contemporary positioning within contemporary 4D digital media.

title: Adv Graphic Design
description: Directed study in which student select subject from any area of commercial design with the goal of developing specific area of expertise. Emphasis on original design and research.

title: Site-Specific Sculpture
description: Emphasis will be on creating sculptures that deal directly with unique spaces and particular locations.  We will focus on how history, culture, and geography inspire site-specific sculptures.

title: Mm/Assemblage Sculpture
description: Combinations of found, fabricated and mixed materials will be assembled into original sculpture compositions.

title: Papermaking/Bookbinding
description: The preparation and processing of fibers for papermaking including experiences in sheet forming,

title: Graphic Design Corp Ident
description: Application of graphic design, including typography, photography and illustrations in developing and implementing identity systems.

title: Intermed Potter's Wheel
description: Continuation of Art 343. The student will master basic wheel and decorative processes developing a personal style in their work.

title: Combined Ceramic Process
description: Exploration of a variety of ceramic building and firing processes such as hand building, wheel and slip casting.

title: Ceramic Mat & Processes
description: Practical and empirical investigation of ceramic materials, techniques and approaches to their use in clay and glazes.

title: Ceramic Sculpture
description: Contemporary ideas and techniques of ceramic fired and unfired sculpture.

title: 2 & 3D Designs for Fabric
description: Exploring the potentialities of fabric as an art experience in two and three dimensional art form.

title: Advanced Ceramics
description: The advanced student will explore individual problems and interests in clay.

title: Motion Design I
description: Introductory topics in time-based media.  Topics may include motion design, animation, video compositing, sound design, interactive design, data visualization, digital storytelling, or related subjects.

title: Motion Design II
description: Advanced topics in time-based media.  Topics may include motion design, animation, video compositing, sound design, interactive design, data visualization, digital storytelling, or related subjects.  (PR: ART 453 )

title: Painting Acrylic & Oil
description: Study and practice of painting in expressing still life, landscape, and the human figure.

title: Painting Acrylic & Oil
description: Study and practice of painting in expressing still life, landscape, and the human figure.

title: Figure Painting
description: Painting the nude model using modern and classical methods.

title: Adv Problems In Painting
description: Refinement and development of individual concerns with content, form and techniques in painting.

title: Digital Drawing and Painting
description: Students will create conceptual illustrations for books, gaming, storyboards and movies by integrating traditional drawing and painting techniques with digital media.

title: History & Phil of Art Ed
description: A survey of the evolution of art education and philosophy, and a study of problems related to art education on the elementary and high school level.

title: Adv Intaglio Printmaking
description: Development of individualized form using intaglio techniques and incorporation multiple colors, plates, assemblages, collagraphs, photo-etching, and mixed media.

title: History of Modern Design
description: History of print and object design from the beginning of the nineteenth through the twentieth century will be studied in terms of art history, technology, politics, economics, and consumer behavior.

title: Lithography
description: Basic techniques of hand lithography, both stone and metal plate.

title: Curr Dev Pub Sch Art K-12
description: Exploring considerations for curriculum development in art education; developing individualized curriculum for specific situations on grade levels K-6 or 7-12.

title: Secondary Ed: Teaching Art
description: This course focuses on instructional standards and methods for teaching art at the secondary level with an emphasis on middle grades. A clinical experience provides observation and teaching.

title: Practicum
description: To be used for learning activities that involve the application of previously learned processes, theories, systems or techniques.

title: Practicum

description: To be used to complete studio specialization and may be repeated. By permission only.

title: Independent Study
description: To be reserved for tutorials, directed and independent readings, directed and independent research, problem reports, and other activities designed to fit the needs of individual students within the major.

title: Independent Study
description: To be reserved for tutorials, directed and independent readings, directed and independent research, problem reports, and other activities designed to fit the needs of individual students with the major.

title: Independent Study
