description: Principles of administration for elementary and secondary school library-media centers and public libraries, including personnel, facilities, budgets, program planning and evaluation, publicity and public relations, audiovisual equipment and materials, computer hardware and software, and methods and materials for teaching library skills.

title: Technology & the Library
description: An introduction to the function, management, and the issues of computer and non-computer technology in the library. Students learn to use technology as an educational tool in the classroom.

title: Pedagogy of New Literacies
description: This course expands on instruction techniques for new literacies in a variety of library types and information-related organizations including the learning needs of K-12, ESL, exceptional and adult learners.

title: Prod of Instruct Tech Material
description: This course will assist students in designing multimedia instructional materials.  Focus will be on the development of web-based instructional materials.

title: Media Skills Dev Inst
description: Basic techniques in producing and using a variety of audiovisual  aids, i.e., photography, scipting,developing loges, audio-recording and layout design for manuals.  This This course also prepares one to produce a slide-tape presentation.

title: Computer Graphics in ITL
description: A basic course designed to assist the student in becoming familiar with a variety of software graphics packages.

title: Senior Seminar
description: A capstone experience for International Affairs majors, intended to provide an integrated perspective on the field through the application of interdisciplinary theories, methods, and practices to global issues.

title: Elementary Japanese I
description: Pronunciation, conversation, reading and composition with emphasis on aural/oral devalopment.  This includes katakana, hiragana, and Chinese characters, used in context.

title: Elementary Japanese II
description: Pronunciation, conversation, reading and composition with emphasis on aural/oral development. This includes katakana, hiragana and Chinese characters, used in context.

title: Intermediate Japanese III
description: Continuation on the intermediate level of the basic skills: pronunciation, conversation, reading, and composition with emphasis on aural/oral development. More work on katakana, hiragana and Chinese characters, used in context.

title: Intermediate Japanese IV
description: Development of practical conversational skills, reading for comprehension, and directed compositions.

title: Japanese Culture (CT)
description: An introduction course of Japanese society and culture through Japanese films, readings, and lectures. This course examines social, political and cultural themes in contemporary Japanese society. Course taught in English.

title: Modern Japanese Lit (CT)
description: Taught in English, course examines Japanese literature from the mid-nineteenth century to the present day.

title: Japanese Anime & Manga (CT)
description: Taught in English, this course examines the history and nature of Japanese animation (anime) and comics (manga).

title: Japanese Special Topics
description: Study of a topic not normally covered in courses.

title: Japanese Special Topics
description: Study of a topic not normally covered in courses.

title: Japanese Special Topics
description: Study of a topic not normally covered in courses.

title: Japanese Special Topics
description: Study of a topic not normally covered in courses.

title: Premodern Japanese Literature
description: This course introduces a comprehensive overview of the history of Japanese literature from the earliest times to the mid-nineteenth century. Course taught in English.

title: Advanced Japanese I
description: Equal emphasis on listening, speaking, reading and writing. Students learn advanced new Kanji characters. The course includes preparation for the Japanese Proficiency Exam. Course taught in Japanese.

title: Japanese Conversation
description: Speaking intensive course designed to develop communicative skills and review language fundamentals acquired in JPN 101 -204 course sequence. Course taught in Japanese.

title: Advanced Japanese II
description: Equal emphasis on listening, speaking, reading, and writing skills. Students learn advanced grammar and 100 Kanji characters. The course includes preparation for the Japanese Proficiency Exam. Course taught in Japanese.

title: Business Japanese
description: Students learn conversational expressions and Japanese manners that can be used in actual business situations in Japanese companies.

title: Japanese Grammar
description: A review of intermediate level of Japanese grammar equivalent to N3 and N4 JLPT (Japanese Language Proficiency Test).  It familiarizes students with test components including vocabulary, reading, grammer, and listening.

title: JPN Society & Culture
description: Am introduction course of Japanese society and culture through Japanese films, readings and lectures. This course examines social, political and cultural themes in contemporary Japanese society. Course taught in Japanese.

title: Readings in Adv Japanese I
description: Students learn comprehensive skills in contemporary Japanese at advanced level and 250 new kanji. Students conduct a presentation in Japanese. Course taught in Japanese.

title: Readings in Adv Japanese II
description: Students continue to learn comprehensive skills in contemporary Japanese at advanced level and 250 new kanji. Students conduct survey research in Japanese. Course taught in Japanese.

title: Japanese Film in English
description: A survey of Japanese cinema from literary,historical, cultural and interdisciplinary perspectives. Readings and lectures introduce the director's work and the backgrounds of individual films. Course taught in English.

title: Lit Asians in Americas
description: Taught in English, this course explores Asian American literature in the U.S., Canada, Latin America, and the Caribbean, focusing on race, class and gender.

title: Japanese Special Topics
description: Study of a topic not normally covered in courses.

title: Japanese Special Topics
description: Study of a topic not normally covered in courses.

title: Japanese Special Topics
description: Study of a topic not normally covered in courses.

title: Japanese Special Topics
description: Study of a topic not normally covered in courses.

title: Japanese Capstone
description: Designed for Japanese majors as a senior capstone seminar. Students develop specific skills to conduct research on chosen topics and to present a research project in Japanese.

title: Fund Jrn Writing & Edit
description: Preparatory course to develop writing and editing skills specifically as related to professional journalism and mass communication.  Required if score is less than 77 on School-administered language-skills test.  Not counted toward graduation.

title: Media Literacy
description: This course equips students with the critical skills needed to navigate, analyze and engage with various forms of media.

title: Media Toolbox
description: Introduction to media writing, creative and critical thinking, information literacy, data management, computer search tools, social media and interviewing for mass communications professionals.

title: Language Use for Media
description: Review of English grammar and usage; preparation for taking SOJMC standard language exam.

title: Introduction to Media Studies
description: This course provides students with an overview of fundamental approaches, theories and perspectives essential to successfully complete a minor in media studies.

title: Intro to Audio Production
description: Fundamentals of audio production, including operation of audio equipment, microphone techniques, digital editing and audio production.  Laboratory work at WMUL-FM is required.

title: Intro to Sports Journalism
description: Introduction to Sports Journalism involves applying the core principles of journalism (honesty, accuracy, clarity, fairness, accountability) to covering any sports related activity.

title: Media Design
description: Design principles; conceive, create and evaluate executions for print, websites, mobile applications, information-based and ad-based graphics.

title: Esports Broadcasting
description: This course provides a comprehensive introduction to the world of esports broadcasting, focusing on the skills, tools, and techniques required to produce professional-quality esports content.

title: Intro to Strategic Comm
description: An examination of theories, practices and techniques of strategic communications used by agencies and organizations.

title: Digital Imaging for JMC
description: Methods of taking and editing still and video digital images for print, broadcast and digital publication.

title: Introduction to Podcasting
description: Students will learn the basics of narrative podcasting, including the history of audio storytelling, branding and listener engagement, production and distribution, script writing, interviewing and editing.

title: Practice in Radio
description: Staff responsibility on campus broadcast facility, WMUL-FM.  (PR:  Permission of instructor and the satisfactory completion of one year of service on WMUL.)

title: Practice in Radio
description: Staff responsibility on campus broadcast facility, WMUL-FM.  (PR:  Permission of instructor and the satisfactory completion of one year of service on WMUL.)

title: Reporting and News Writing
description: Techniques of cross media news writing designed to develop basic skills necessary for beginning reporters and public relations professionals through in-class laboratory experience.

title: Beat Reporting
description: Practice in gathering and writing news.  Emphasis placed on beat assignment reporting, interviewing techniques, and specialized reporting.  A laboratory class in which students write for student media.

title: Advanced Editing & Design
description: Advanced course in copy editing, headline writing and design for daily, community and public relations newspapers/newsletters.  Laboratory instruction in the printed and online issues of the university newspaper.

title: Sports News Reporting
description: In-depth news reporting on the sports beat, breaking news and analysis.

title: Copy Editing
description: Copy editing for newspapers, magazines and online, with emphasis on finding and correcting errors of fact, grammar, spelling. AP style; writing headlines; selecting and placing stories in publications.

title: Sportscasting
description:
A "hands-on" course designed to develop announcing, interviewing and writing skills
specifically as such skills relate to play-by-play techniques and practices.

title: AD & PR Principles and Ethics
description: An examination of advertising and public relations theories and practices used by organizations across all sectors of society.  Special emphasis on the role of ethics in advertising and public relations.

title: Radio-TV Announ & Newscasting
description: Specialized training in the interpretive skills of announcing and newscasting.

title: Intro to Video Production
description: Introduction to the fundamentals of video production, dealing with cameras, microphones, lighting, staging, field Production, editing, post production.

title: Basic Broadcast News
description: Introduction and overview of electronic journalism. Emphasis on broadcast writing. Students will report for Newscenter 88 WMUL-FM.

title: Esports Shoutcasting
description: Explores esports shoutcasting, including live commentary, storytelling techniques, production skills, and industry professionalism. Emphasizes practical experience with tools such as OBS while preparing students for careers in esports broadcasting.

title: Mass Comm Law and Ethics
description: A cross-media overview combining study of legal concepts important to the media practitioner, ethical conflicts encountered, and application of legal precedents and ethical principles.

title: Digital Presence
description: Digital strategies in news and strategic communication contexts including digital presence and content development; ethical issues and best practices.

title: Advanced Podcasting
description: Students learn advanced podcasting techniques, music and sound effects, audio mixing, mastering, and multi-track production techniques and explore legal issues such as intellectual property and copyrights permissions.

title: Practice in Radio or TV
description: Staff responsibility on campus broadcast facilities. (PR: Permission of instructor)

title: Practice in Radio or TV
description: Staff responsibility on campus broadcast facilities. (PR: Permission of instructor)

title: AD & PR and Continuity Writing
description: Crafting of multimedia content for advertising-public relations and broadcast continuity with attention to communications strategy development, script formats and writing styles for traditional, digital and social media.

title: Content Creation
description: Concept-driven media design with an emphasis on digital media.

title: Media Sales & Underwriting
description: Introduction to business side of mass media with emphasis on sales in television and radio; consideration of competing media; differences between commercial and public broadcasting; and the elements of underwriting.

title: Digital Imaging II
description: Advanced methods of taking and editing still and video images for print broadcast, and online publications.

title: Law of Mass Communication
description: A cross-media overview of the legal concepts important to the media professional with special focus on the roles, rights and responsibilities of those individuals.

title: Hist Am JRN & Mass Comm
description: The development of mass communication in the United States, including journalism, broadcasting, public relations and advertising, and the relation of media history to political, economic and social trends in America.

title: Creative Branding
description: Creative direction in developing the personality, reputation, appearance and character of a great brand consistent in all aspects.

title: Research Analytics
description: Introduction to methods and tools used to monitor, inform and evaluate advertising & public relations decisions including applications of research methods, data analysis and social and digital media analytics.

title: Magazine Editorial Prac
description: Study of the organization and functions of the magazine editorial department, with practice in planning magazine content, laying out pages and establishing production procedures.

title: Reporting Public Affairs
description: Advanced instruction involving in-depth cross media reporting on operations of government, nonprofit organizations and some social issues. Special emphasis on understanding and explaining their public statements, budgets and finances.

title: Advertising Strategy
description: Students will analyze advertising problems, propose strategic solutions, create messages and present strategies to a client. Students will write, design and produce advertising for a variety of media.

title: Electronic Media Mgt
description: Covers special circumstances faced by electronic media managers including programming, audience development, legal constraints, employment practices, technological developments, social pressures and other concerns.

title: Media Strategy
description: Introduction to research and planning for advertising & public relations message distribution including audience analysis, media analytics and evaluation metrics for paid, owned and earned traditional, digital and social media.

title: Magazine Article Writing
description: Fundamentals of researching and writing the popular, factual magazine article; techniques of selling articles to magazines.

title: Collective 37
description: Collective 37, Marshall University’s student-run communications agency, provides the opportunity to apply skills learned in other courses to complete work for clients. Course open to all majors. Permission only.

title: AD-PR Video Production
description: Students will create and produce various informational, promotional, and training video for companies and organizations.  Students will learn to make and critique AD-PR videos that inform, persuade, and entertain.  PR: JMC 260

title: Advanced Video
description: Development of the elements necessary for the production of detailed video projects.  Students study the creation and production of public affairs, educational and creative video programming.

title: International Mass Comm
description: Development of various systems of mass communications and comparison with the United States.

title: Public Relations Planning
description: Public relations planning, writing, and pitching of strategies and tactics to clients.

title: Public Relations Case Studies
description: Analysis of public relations problems, issues and crises experienced by business, non-profit, government, education, and healthcare organizations, with particular emphasis on ethical problem solving and crisis elimination.

title: AD & PR Campaign Management
description: Students function as an AD & PR agency that researches, plans, implements and evaluates advertising and public relations campaigns for clients.

title: Contemp Issues Radio-TV
description: An examination of the current political, social, economic and legal issues affecting the decision making process in the newsrooms and programming centers of the electronic media.  (PR: jr standing)

title: Television Reporting
description: Students report, shoot, edit, write, produce and anchor MU Report, a student-produced newscast.  The class makes use of the university's television studio.

title: Advanced TV Reporting
description: Students report, shoot, edit, write, produce and anchor MU Report, a student-produced newscast, on an advanced level. The class makes use of the university's television studio.

title: Race Gender & Mass Media
description: A seminar that explores the portrayals and participation of women and people of color in the mass media.

title: Emerging Media for Journalists
description: Students will demonstrate uses of emerging media for journalistic purposes, focusing on social networks and conversational media with associated digital media tools and platforms.

title: Web Design for Mass Media
description: Creative and practical aspects of typography, design and interactivity of online communications for the mass media.

title: Multimedia Reporting
description: An advanced laboratory reporting class in which students, in teams and individually, produce multimedia stories including audio, video, still photos, text and graphics for publication. (Capstone)

title: Environmental Mass Comm
description: Identifying, analyzing and discussing issues that challenge environmental communications, strategizing the creation of comprehensible environmental messages, and examining philosophies that underpin environmental communications.

title: Professional Practicum
description: Instruction to assist students in meeting career expectations.  Short-term courses designed to bridge instructional programs and practices of professional journalism.  Students may participate in supervised publications work in reporting, editing and advertising.

title: Documentary Journalism
description: Students will view, critique and evaluate the genres of nonfiction storytelling. Students will produce an original 15-minute video to be screened to the public.

title: Special Topics
description: Independent or group studies or projects under the direction of a faculty member. Includes regular meetings with the instructor. Projects may include mass communication research or group field projects.

title: Special Topics
description: Independent or group studies or projects under the direction of a faculty member. Includes regular meetings with the instructor. Projects may include mass communication research or group field projects.

title: Jrn & Mass Comm Internship I
description: Supervised mass communications work as a professional journalist/communicator for a media company, communications agency or communications division in an organization or business. Conference with internship coordinator for internship evaluation.

title: Jrn & Mass Comm Intern II
description: Supervised mass communications work as a professional professional journalist/communicator for a media company, communications agency or communications division in an organization or business. Conference with internship coordinator for internship evaluation.

title: Professional Portfolio
description: Students will assess their work and prepare artifacts for their professional portfolio and program review.

title: Love & Hate in Roman Republic
description: Students will review and apply their knowledge of Latin vocabulary and grammar to develop translation skills through reading Latin poetry and prose and examining its relationship to Roman culture.

title: Ghosts & Monsters in Anc. Rome
description: Students will review and apply their knowledge of Latin vocabulary and grammar to develop translation skills through reading Latin poetry and prose and examining its relationship to Roman culture.

title: Julius Caesar: Man and Legend
description: Students will review and apply their knowledge of Latin vocabulary and grammar to develop translation skills through reading Latin poetry and prose and examining its relationship to Roman culture.

title: Roman Festival and Spectacle
description: Students will review and apply their knowledge of Latin vocabulary and grammar to develop translation skills through reading Latin poetry and prose and examining its relationship to Roman culture.

title: Conversational Latin I
description: Introduction to basic skills of oral comprehension, composition and pronunciation of Latin.

title: Conversational Latin II
description: Introduction to basic skills of oral comprehension, composition and pronunciation of Latin.

title: Conversational Latin III
description: Introduction to basic skills of oral comprehension, composition, and pronunciation of Latin.

title: Catullus
description: A close reading in Latin of the poetry of Catullus with consideration of its literary antecedents and its importance to Roman Literature.

title: Readings in Ovid
description: Close reading in Latin of selections from Ovid's erotic and epic poetry.

title: Sallust and Nepos
description: A close reading in Latin of selected works from Sallust and Nepos.

title: Latin Prose Comp: Tacitus
description:
Study of Latin Prose Composition, the translation of English to Latin, with special 
reference to the style, syntax and vocabulary of Tacitus.

title: Roman Elegy: Prop & Tib
description: Close readings in Latin of selections from the elegies of Propertius and Tibullus.

title: Roman Epistolary Lit
description: A unique look at Roman life, public and private, through a close reading in Latin of the correspondence of Cicero, Seneca the Elder, and Pliny the Younger. Younger.

title: Roman Satire
description: Close readings in Latin of selections from the satires of Horace and Juvenal and the epigrams of Martial.

title: Tacitus: Annals Agricola
description: A close reading in Latin of selections from the works of Tacitus.

title: Latin Prose Comp: Survey
description: Study of Latin Prose Composition, the translation of English into Latin, with special reference to the style, syntax and vocabulary of the 1st c BCE.

title: Independent Study
description: Non-Latin majors may enroll in Latin Independent Study courses for one hour credit to meet general requirements in literature.  For such students instruction and readings will be entirely in English.  Consult chairman for current offerings.

title: Independent Study
description: Non-Latin majors may enroll in Latin Independent Study courses for one hour credit to meet general requirements in literature.  For such students instruction and readings will be entirely in English.  Consult chariman for current offerings.

title: Independent Study
description: Non-Latin majors may enroll in Latin Independent Study courses for one hour credit to meet general requirements in literature.  For such students instruction and readings will be entirely in English.  Consult chairman for current offerings.

title: Independent Study
description: Non-Latin majors may enroll in Latin Independent Study courses for one hour credit to meet general requirements in literature.  For such students instruction and readings will be entirely in English.  Consult chairman for current offerings.

title: Classical Language Capstone
description: Students prepare for their career path by developing a paper that uses professional tools and scholarship; create personal statements and a resume.

title: Legal Environ of Business
description: Law and the judicial system.  the relationship of law, government, ethics and the consumer of business enterprise.  The student of contracts, torts, government regulation of business, environmental and consumer protection.

title: Commercial Law
description: A continuation of Accounting 207.  Emphasizes in-depth case study of the law of commercial paper, business organizations security, and real and personal property.

title: Legal Aspts Hlth Care Org
description: A survey of basic legal problems facing a hospital administrator.  The study also includes constitutional and administrative law issues dealing with medicaid and medicare and regional planning.

title: Entrep Law & Ethics
description:
Students will examine the basic legal and ethical issues involving the creation, maintenance,
and expansion of small businesses.

title: Tourism and Hospitality Law
description: A review of the statutes and regulations as related to the hospitality, entertainment, and tourism industries including hotel/guest relationships, franchise agreements, and commercial law.

title: Special Topics in LE
description: Study of an advanced topic not normally covered in other courses.

title: Introduction to Business
description: Career exploration and preparation.  Emphasis on modern supervisory management techniques in various business fields.

title: Business Quantitative Methods
description: This course introduces statistical concepts and tools that can be leveraged for business data analytics, covering hypothesis testing, regression analysis, decision-making, time series analysis, and process control.

title: Management Analytics
description: The course will provide a framework for both the analytical tools and specific managerial problems, emphasizing model development and the use of tools to manage, analyze, and visualize data.

title: Principles of Management
description: A comprehensive survey of the fundamental principles of management applicable to all forms of organizations.  The course provides the student with a basis for thinking about complex business situations in the framework of analysis of the management process.  Some case analysis of management problems used.

title: Project Management
description: It covers key components of project management- project integration, scope, and time management; cost analysis, quality control, human resource considerations; risk management; procurement and contract management; and planning and scheduling.

title: Hospitality & Tourism Mgt
description: An introduction to the hospitality industry, emerging trends and various management functions related to it.

title: Aviation Management Safety
description: This course provides students with a comprehensive coverage of aviation safety and security.  The course examines topics related to the latest procedures, flight technologies, challenges, and accident statistics.

title: Health Care Management
description: An introduction to the management of health care organizations.

title: Health Care Delivery Systems
description: An examination of the various private and public sector health care delivery systems currently management.

title: Health Care Prods & Services
description: Application of business principles as they apply to the management of health care products and services in today's health care environment.

title: Energy Management Principles
description: A managerial perspective of regional, national and international energy issues.  Course provides an objective assessment into the projections, limitations, costs, and tradeoffs associated with conventional and alternative sources of energy.

title: Principles of Renewable Energy
description: An introduction to renewable energy management, markets, and sustainable business solutions.  Nuclear, hydro, solar, wind, geothermal, biomass and biofuel sectors will be evaluated for their economic viability and business profitability.

title: Business & Society
description: An examination of the manager's social and environmental responsibilities to his employees, customers, and the general public, and other external factors which management must be cognizant of in modern society.

title: Operations Management
description: Management of operation systems including system design, implementation and control.  Analysis of the system in the areas of product, process, material quality, and facilities management.  Topics include breakeven analysis, inventory models, transportation models, network analysis.

title: Supply Chain Sustainability
description: Understanding the value, issues and trends regarding sustainability in modern, complex and global supply chains and business practices, by appreciating the concepts of sustainability, circular economy, and supply chain processes.

title: Organizational Behavior
description: An interdisciplinary examination of the nature of individuals, groups and organizations and organizations and their interactions.

title: Organizational Development
description: An examination of the dynamics of change within organizations.  The course will examine the cause of resistance to change and purposeful methodologies for implementing change including behavioral, technological, and structural in attempt to describe a holistic approach.

title: Human Resource Management
description: Analyze the role of Human Resource Managers within strategic decision making.  Topics include: selection, training, assessment, compensation and employee relations.  Current topics also covered.

title: Industrial Relations
description: A study of labor-management relations in union and non-union settings.  Topics include: conflict resolution techniques, negotiation strategy, partitipative management, and labor theory.  Labor laws and history are reviewed.

title: Negotiations
description: The theory and practice of negotiation in organizational settings including negotiation strategies and their impact on the outcomes achieved. Students will build negotiation skills through experintial exercises and cases.

title: Leadership
description: Leadership styles, principles, models, and practical applications including: motivation and communication, teamwork, use of power, development of trust, effective group facilitation, negotiation and persuasion, effective change, and ethics.

title: Diversity Issues in Business
description: Students will gain insights into primary and secondary dimensions of diversity, and be able to describe pertinent ethical, legal, media and marketing issues.  They will improve organizations' management of diversity

title: International Management
description: Focuses on the economic, political, legal, technological, and cultural issues faced by international managers. Topics include developing cultural awareness, implementing global strategy, and competing with ethical integrity.

title: Green Management
description: Focuses on environmental sustainability and the practices involved in managing a green business, including: eco-advantage strategies, supply chains, preventing the failure of eco-initiatives and sustained competitive advantage.

title: Hlth Care Policy Seminar
description: An integrative discussion course on current problems and future policies and strategies as they are related to facilities planning and utilization, staffing and organization and providing quality health care to community.

title: Energy Management Strategy
description: Comprehensive coverage of all facets of the management of energy sources, production, risks, and markets.  Provides managerial tools necessary to increase production capabilities and maneuver within the transforming energy sectors.

title: Aviation Management Capstone
description: This course culminates the student's entire learning experience in the Aviation Management major.  It involves case discussions, readings, and simulations.  Students will complete a comprehensive, semester-long project.

title: Strategic Management
description: The integrative capstone course concerning theory and practice of top managements plans to attain outcomes consistent wit the organization's mission and goals including strategy formulation, implementation and control.

title: New Venture Dynamics
description: Managing small enterprises, as opposed to large corporations. In cooperation with the Small Business Administration. Students work as trainee management consultants with small businesses in the area.

title: Prac Health Care Mgt I
description: Field experience in management of Health Care Organizations.

title: Import Export Management
description: Students will learn the general and specific information relevant to conducting export business from the United States to different parts of the world.

title: Special Topics
description: Study of an advanced topic not normally covered in other courses.  Management majors only, with permission of department chairman.

title: Internship
description: A supervised internship in which the student works for a business firm/agency to gain practical experience in the student's major.  The program of work and study will be defined in advance and the student performance will be evaluated.

title: Bus Computer Applications
description: An introduction to computer applications in business, including spreadsheets, databases, presentation and word processing. Students also learn to integrate spreadsheet and database outputs into presentations and reports.

title: Prin Management Info Systems
description: Introduction to the development, selection, use, and impact of information and communication technologies and systems in modern organizations and enterprises.

title: Intro to Business Programming
description: Introduction to programming in a business context, emphasizing problem solving using basic programming logic and data structures, interface concepts, file and database access, and selection and use of development tools. (PR: MIS 290 )

title: Bus Sys Analysis & Design
description: The course covers business application systems development, behavioral considerations in the: development process, feasibility assessment, requirement analysis, and communication skills.  Emphasis on prototyping and fourth generation languages. (PR: Passing the COB Computer Literacy Test and any programming language)

title: Bus Telecomm Sys
description: To understand the applications, concepts and management of telecommunications. Students will be exposed to network components and network operations. Emphasis will be on strategic business applications of telecommunication systems.

title: Intro to Database Mgt Systems
description: Introduction to enterprise data administration emphasizing database environment and architecture, relational model and languages, database requirements, and modeling.  Introduction to the use of a database managment system.

title: Intro to Bus Intel & Analytics
description: Introduction to the field of business intelligence & analytics, introducing the use of big data, statistical, quantitative analysis, exploratory and predictive models, and fact-based management to drive decisions and actions.

title: MIS Practicum
description: Students explore career interests, while applying classroom knowledge and skills, gaining a clearer sense of what they still need to learn and an opportunity to build professional networks.

title: Info Security Management
description:
A study of information security risk analysis and assessment; threats to information security;
defense measures; and legal, privacy, and ethical issues in information security.

title: Business Sys Proj Mgt
description: Project management for information and process-oriented organizational systems.  Tools, techniques, feasibility, post-project evaluation, information and knowledge exchange, change and vendor management. (PR:290/permission of COB)

title: Strat Management Info Systems
description: A capstone course for management majors. Emphasis will be on creating and using information systems to give businesses a competitive advantage and provide strategic support for all levels of management.

title: Principles of Selling
description: Elements of professional personal selling from prospecting through follow-up designed for individuals preparing for a career in sales/marketing and those desiring skills to influence, persuade, or lead others.

title: MKT Concepts and Applications
description: Introduction to marketing as the central activity of organizations in creating exchanges with customers. Focuses on strategies related to environmental opportunities and threats using product, price, promotion and distribution tools.

title: Integrated MKT Communications
description: A managerial analysis of the principles and practices of the promotion mix from the viewpoints of the consumer, the firm, the industry, and the macroenvironment.

title: Retail Management
description: Management of retail establishments including successful retail merchandising, stock control, buying, pricing, marketing, advertising, promotion, displaying, credit, and selling of goods and/or services.

title: Hospitality & Tour Marketing
description: Acquaint students with the marketing functions within the hospitality and tourism industry to develop effective marketing plans and gain an understanding of hospitality and tourism consumer behavior.

title: Prin Domestic Transport
description: Introduction to the history, economics, and regulation of U.S. domestic motor, rail, air and pipline transportation. Particular emphasis is placed upon the significance of transporation to the development of the United States and today's economy.

title: Supply Chain Logistics
description: A supply chain approach is used to explain activities that create an efficient flow of products from point of origin to point of consumption in order to satisfy customer requirements.

title: International Marketing
description: A study of marketing across national borders.  Emphasis is placed on international environments, methods of entry, and marketing mix development, including the conflict between standardization and adaptation.

title: Business to Business Marketing
description: Study of marketing products and services to business, institutions and government. Focus on organizational buying, market planning and development of marketing mix. (Prerequisite: MKT 340 )

title: Social Media Marketing
description: Social media as a marketing function: Students will gain the knowledge and skills necessary to engage and service the customer and explore the strategic use of current social media platforms.

title: Marketing Analytics
description: Students analyze data to make marketing decisions regarding segmentation and target market selection, product positioning, brand choice, customer relationship management, pricing, customer lifetime value, and other areas.

title: Digital Marketing
description: Introduction to Google AdWords, Google Analytics, search engine optimization (SEO), website design, content and email marketing.

title: Internet Marketing
description: Introduction to Internet as a sales and marketing tool, web page development, strategic planning for e-commerce, non-Internet functions which support e-commerce, and integration of e-commerce into conventional MKT mix.

title: Consumer Behavior
description: Acquaints the student with individual and group behavior as it pertains to consumer activity. Theories and findings in the behavioral sciences, as well as those set forth by the marketing scholars, are examined so as to understand the behavioral patterns of consumers. Cultural, social, and psychological influences are considered, in addition to the traditional economic interpretations. The stress of the course is on incorporating these data into the managing of the marketing effort.

title: Sales Management
description: An  exploration of the duties and activities of sales managers.  Topics typically include planning and forecasting as well as organizing, staffing, training, compensating, motivating, and evaluating the sales force. (PR: MKT 340 )

title: Market Research
description: Scope and importance of market and distribution research; product, package, brand analysis and social impact; consumer, industrial and institutional surveys, quantitative and qualitative analysis of market data; situation analysis, sampling, tabulation and presentation methods.

title: Services Marketing
description: Examination of the marketing of services offered by business and non-business organizations with particular emphasis on the unique aspect of the services marketing mix and the implementation of service strategy. (Prerequisite: MKT 340 )

title: Strategic Marketing
description: Capstone integrated study of marketing for decision making. Emphasis on the application of marketing principles and concepts for the purpose of developing, analyzing, and mofifying marketing plans and strategy. (PR: MKT 340 and Senior Standing).

title: Special Topics
description: Study of an advanced topic not normally covered in other courses. Marketing majors only, with permission of department chairman.

title: Internship
description: A supervised experience in which the student works for a business firm/agency to gain practical experience by completing a defined work program.  Student performance is evaluated.  This course may not be used as a marketing elective.

title: Prep for College Math B
description: A mastery-based course that will prepare students for college algebra.

title: Abr Prep for College Math B
description: An abridged mastery-based course that will prepare students for College Algebra.

title: Concepts and Applications (CT)
description: Critical thinking course for non-science majors that develops quantitative reasoning skills. Topics include logical thinking, problem-solving, linear modeling, beginning statistics and probability, exponential and logarithmic models, formula use, and financial concepts.

title: Cncpts & Apps-Expanded (CT)
description: Critical thinking course for non-science majors that develops quantitative reasoning skills.  Topics include logical thinking, linear modeling, statistics and probability, exponential and logarithmic modeling, and financial concepts, with arithmetic review.

title: Plane Trigonometry
description: Definitions of circular functions; graphs of the trigonometric functions, trigonometric identities, and applications.

title: College Algebra-Expanded
description: A brief but careful review of the main techniques of algebra.  Polynomials, rational, exponential, and logarithmic functions.  Graphs, equations and inequalities, sequences.

title: College Algebra
description: Polynomial, rational, exponential, and logarithmic functions.  Graphs, equations and inequalities, sequences.

title: Precalculus with Sci Applica
description: Functions used in calculus including polynomial, rational, exponential, logarithmic, and trigonometric. Systems of equations and inequalities, conic sections, polar and parametric equations, sequences and series, Binomial Theorem.

title: Applied Calculus
description: A brief survey of calculus including both differentiation and integration with applications.

title: Applied Calculus Honors
description: A brief survey of calculus including both differentiation and integration with applications.  This honors course will also introduce topics from differential equations with applications.

title: Applied Math Reasoning (CT)
description: A critical thinking course in applied mathematical reasoning.  Topics include logic, problem solving, linear modeling, beginning statistics and probability, exponential and logarithmic modeling, formula use.

title: Discrete Structures
description: Sets, relations, directed and undirected graphs, monoids, groups, lattices, Boolean algebra, and propositional logic.

title: Calculus/Analytic Geom I (CT)
description: An introduction to calculus and analytic geometry, emphasizing critical thinking.  Limits, derivatives, and integrals of the elementary functions of one variable, including transcendental functions.

title: Calculus I Honors (CT)
description: An introduction to calculus and analytic geometry for honors students, emphasizing critical thinking.  Limits, derivatives, and integrals of the elementary functions of one variable, including transcendental functions.

title: Calculus/Analytic Geom II
description: A study of the conics and transcendental functions, techniques of integration, improper integrals, indeterminate forms and infinite series.

title: Calculus/Analytic Geom III
description: Analytic geometry of two and three dimensions, partial differentiation, and multiple integrals.

title: Intro to Higher Math
description: A transition between elementary calculus and higher mathematics with emphasis on techniques of proofs.

title: Math for Secondary Educators
description: An integrated study of proof, number, quantity, measurement, algebra, trigonometry, and geometry for secondary mathematics educators. Includes teaching techniques, engagement strategies, assessment analysis, and topics specific to professional educators.

title: Math for Secondary Educators 2
description: An integrated study of proof, probability, statistics, functions, trigonometry, pre-calculus, and calculus for secondary mathematics educators. Includes teaching techniques, engagement strategies, assessment analysis, and topics specific to professional educators.

title: Elementary Linear Algebra
description: Systems of linear equations, matrices and determinants, vector spaces, linear transformations, eigenvalues, eigenvectors, and applications.

title: Linear Algebra
description: Vector spaces, matrices and determinants, linear transformations, eigenvalues and eigenvectors, and applications.

title: Ordinary Diff Equations
description: A study of differential equations, their solutions, and applications to physical systems, emphasizing closed-form solving methods.  Laplace transforms, orthogonal functions, approximation and numerical methods with applications.

title: Intro to Complex Variables
description: An introductory survey of complex numbers, analytic functions, properties of elementary functions, integrals, series, residues and poles, with a focus on practical applications.

title: Vector Calculus
description: A course in n-dimensional calculus: the derivative, the integral, and applications.  Coordinate-free methods are emphasized.

title: Structure of Algebra
description: Emphasis on the language of Modern Elementary Algebra. Recommended for pre-service elementary teachers and for elementary and secondary in-service teachers.  May not be used for either a degree offered by the Department of Mathematics or for a 7-9 or 7-12 mathematics specialization.

title: Structure Modern Geometry
description: Informal development of geometry.  Recommended for preservice elementary teachers and for elementary and secondary inservice teachers.  May not be used for either a degree offered by the Department of Mathematics or for a 7-9 or 7-12 mathematics specialization.

title: Math Methods and Materials
description: Content and content-specific pedagogy for secondary mathematics education majors.

title: History of Mathematics
description: A study of the history of mathematics from the time of the ancient Greeks to the end of the nineteenth century.

title: Mathematical Modeling
description: Students work in teams to construct mathematical models of various real-world situations.  Problems to be modeled are drawn from diverse areas of application and use a wide range of undergraduate mathematics.

title: Partial Differential Equations
description: Differential equations.  Heat Equation, Laplace's Equation, separation of variables, Fourier series, vibrating strings, eigenvalue problems, finite differences, Bessel functions, Legendre polynomials.

title: Advanced Differential Equation
description: Differential equations are studied qualitatively. Topics include the existence and uniqueness of solutions and the behavior of solutions including stability of nonlinear systems, periodic solutions, and approximation using pertubation methods.

title: Advanced Calculus I
description: The number system, limits, sequences, partial differentiation with applications, maxima and minima of functions of several variables.  Theory of definite integrals, multiple integrals, line and surface integrals, improper integrals, infinite series.

title: Advanced Calculus II
description: The number system, limits, sequences, partial differentiation with applications, maxima and minima of functions of several variables.  Theory of definite integrals, multiple integrals, line and surface integrals, improper integrals, infinite series.

title: Topology I
description:
First course in topology.  Basics of point-set topology: metric and topological spaces, continuity,
connectedness, compactness, products, quotients.  Surfaces and simplicial complexes,
Euler characteristics.

title: Topology II
description: First course in algebraic topology.   Homotopy, fundamental group, simplicial homology.

title: Graph Th and Combinatorics
description: This course is designed to introduce students in mathematical sciences to the theorems, techniques, and applications of graph theory and combinatorics.

title: Numerical Linear Algebra
description: Direct and iterative methods for numerical solution of linear systems of equations. Eigenvalues and eigenvectors. Error Analysis and norms.  Related topics.

title: Numerical Analysis
description: The theory and technique of numerical computation involving the difference calculus, the summation calculus, interpolation methods, solution of systems of equations, and methods of solution of ordinary differential equations.

title: Modern Geometries
description: Finite geometrics, basic background material for the modern development of Euclidean Geometry, other geometries.

title: Projective Geometry
description: Projective Geometry using both synthetic and algebraic methods.

title: Modern Algebra I
description: Structure of the abstract mathematical systems; groups, rings, fields, with illustrations and applications from number theory.

title: Modern Algebra II
description: Continuation of MTH 450 .

title: Number Theory and Cryptography
description: A survey of number theory and cryptography topics. Topics include divisibility, modular arithmetic, quadratic residues, and quadratic reciprocity, hashing algorithms, cipher methods,  and their cryptanalysis.

title: Mathematical Cryptography
description: Mathematical Cryptography provides an overview of algebraic structures, elliptic curves, linear codes, and additional cryptography topics.

title: Internship in Mathematics
description: A supervised internship in an area of mathematics, applied mathematics, or statistics.  By permission only.

title: Senior Seminar
description: Capstone experience in reading, doing writing and speaking mathematics.  Student will explore topics related to a theme chosen by the instructor.

title: Mech Engineering Computations
description: Introduction to effective problem-solving techniques used in various engineering applications. Computational tools including C and MATLAB will e covered.

title: Manufacturing Processes
description: An introduction to manufacturing systems and strategy.  A study of manufacturing processes.  Measurement and quality assurance machining, welding, and casting processes.  Hot and cold forming and joining processes. 1 lec and 6 lab

title: Circuits and Instrumentation
description: This course provides basic concepts of DC and AC circuit analysis, an overview of the instrument characteristics and measurement principles, and description and evaluation of sensors commonly used with instrumentation.

title: Intro. to Aircraft Systems
description: This course covers an introduction to different systems involved in the successful flight of an aircraft.

title: Thermodynamics II
description: Gas, vapor, combined power cycles, co-generation, entropy, combustion, fuel cells, and equations of state.

title: Flight Mechanics
description: This course covers flight dynamics; modeling, stability, and control aspects of aircrafts.

title: Experimental Design
description: Design and analysis of experiments with emphasis on applications in mechanical engineering. Introduction to statistical analysis, measurement principles, experimental data analysis and testing, error & uncertainty analysis, and curve fitting.

title: Fluid Power
description: This course covers physical principles of fluid power cylinders, control valves, fluid power components: compressors, pumps, valves, cylinders, and motors, fluid power circuits, troubleshooting: hydraulic, symptoms, procedures, pneumatics.

title: Thermo-Fluid Lab
description: Experiments in fluid, heat, and thermodynamics; Principles and performance of measuring systems; Laboratory experience; Technical report writing.

title: Manufacturing Methods/Design
description: This course covers economical production by understanding the capabilities of different manufacturing processes, candidate manufacturing processes for a given part, performing manufacturabiliity evaluation at the design stage, automation, IMS.

title: Machine Element Design
description: Mechanical design of machine elements, static and fatigue failures, shaft systems, bearings, gears, springs, screws and fasteners.

title: Heat Transfer
description: Analysis and solutions of conduction, free and forced convection, radiation heat transfer and design of heat exchangers.

title: Fluid Dynamics
description: Fluid statics, fluid kinematics, Bernoulli equation, momentum analysis, dimensional analysis, internal flows, fluid machinery, power and efficiency, external flows, compressible flows, differential analysis and Navier-Stokes equation, computational fluid dynamics.

title: Kinematics & Design of Machine
description: The determination of the motion and forces of machines and mechanisms including rotating machinery, cams and gears.  Analyze position, velocity, accelerations, static loads, and dynamic loads.

title: Control Systems
description: This course provides basic concepts of control; open and closed-loop control systems; and PLC Programming.

title: Flight Stability and Control
description: This course covers flight dynamics; and modeling, stability, and control aspects of aircrafts.

title: Solid Mechanics Lab
description: Engineering measurements and experimentations.  Hands-on labs and data analyses in several major topics of the Mechanics of Materials theory and Theory of Machines.

title: Design of Thermal Systems
description: Design and analysis of thermal systems including components selection and integrations.

title: Design of Mechanical System
description: Problem solving methodology in the design, analysis, and synthesis of mechanical systems.  Engineering design process involving modeling, computer simulation, concepts of optimization, robustness, reliability, sustainability.

title: Design & Analysis Energy Sys
description: Design characteristics and operational performance of energy systems.

title: Hydraulic & Pneumatic Control
description: This course covers standard symbols, pumps, control valves, assemblies, actuators, filter regulator lubricator (FRL), maintenance procedures, switching, control devices, fluid power circuits including design, application, and troubleshooting.

title: Engineering Analysis
description: Experimental laboratory mainly from within the thermo-fluids area, concerned with fluid statics, flow, heat transfer, internal combustion engines, data acquistion, analysis, including use of computers.  Principles of good experimental design.

title: CNC and Rapid Prototyping
description: This course covers CNC CAD/CAM, CNC tools, coordinate systems, CNC programming Language, CNC operation, CNC tool paths, CNC turning, G/M code reference, CNC milling work-holding, rapid prototyping, 3D printing.

title: Jet Propulsion
description: The theories and principles of jet propulsion.  Thermodynamic cycles.  The mechanics and thermodynamics of combustion.  Turbine engine performance characteristics.  Component and cycle analysis of jet engines and turbomachinery.

title: Capstone Design I
description: Prepares mechanical engineering students for ME 453 Capstone Design II, professional responsibility, development of effective communication skills, and learning strategies.  Students begin to work on a capstone design project.

title: Capstone Design II
description: Students utilize the engineering design process to complete a comprehensive project that addresses a real-world problem with realistic constraints in a collaborative environment.

title: Metallurgy
description: Covers material properties and behavior of pure metals and common metal alloys.  Discuss various aspects of extractive, mechanical, physical metallurgy, theory and practice of identification, selection, processing, conditioning, and testing.

title: Materials for Aerospace
description: Covers the main structural materials used in aircraft in terms of their production, properties, performance, and applications.

title: Vibrations
description: Modeling of vibratory motion of single and multiple degree of freedom systems; free and forced response; modal summation method for response predictions; simulation of the vibration by using Matlab.

title: Mechatronics
description: Dynamic analysis of mechatronic systems, sensors, transducers, and electric circuits and control.

title: Finite Elements Method
description: This course covers flight dynamics; and modeling, stability, and control aspects of aircrafts.

title: Computational Fluid Dynamics
description: Conservation laws of fluid motion and heat transfer; Fundamental knowledge to use numerical techniques to solve fluid-thermal problems.

title: Introduction to Robotics
description: Introduction to effective problem-solving techniques used in various engineering applications.  Computational tools including C and MATLAB will be covered.

title: Special Topics
description: Subject matter to be selectred from topics of current interest.

title: Special Topics
description: Subject matter to be selected from topics of current interest.

title: Special Topics
description: Subject matter to be selected from topics of current interest.

title: Special Topics

title: Independent Study
description: Individual study of advanced mechanical engineering areas.

title: Independent Study
description: Individual study of advanced mechanical engineering areas.

title: Independent Study
description: Individual study of advanced mechanical engineering areas.

title: Independent Study
description: Individual study of advanced mechanical engineering areas.

title: Intro to Radiography
description: Provides an overview of the foundations in radiography and the practitioner's role in the health care delivery system and professional responsibilities of the radiographer.

title: Patient Care Img Science
description: Content is designed to identify the role of the radiographer in patient care, including consideration for the physical and psychological needs of the patient and family.

title: Radiographic Anatomy
description: Content is designed to introduce the student to radiographic anatomy. Emphasis is placed on identifying structures visible on correctly performed radiographic procedures.

title: Imaging Procedures I
description: Content is designed to provide the knowledge base necessary to perform standard imaging procedures. Students will practice imaging procedures in lab prior to performing them on patients.

title: Clinical Practice I
description: Content and clinical practice experiences are designed to sequentially develop, apply, critically analyze, integrate, synthesize and evaluate concepts and theories in the performance of radiologic procedures.

title: Imaging Procedures II
description: Content is designed to provide the knowledge base necessary to perform special imaging procedures and basic computed tomography.

title: Pharmacology for Imaging Sci
description: Content is designed to provide basic concepts of pharmacology including delivery of and pharmacodynamics associated with imaging contrast media.

title: Intro to Imaging Equip
description: Content is designed to provide knowledge in radiographic, fluoroscopic, mobile and tomographic equipment requirements and design.

title: Clinical Practice II
description: Content is designed to provide the knowledge base necessary to perform standard imaging procedures. Students will begin clinical rotations in computed tomography.

title: Seminar Imaging Science
description: Introduces student to current research in imaging science. Emphasis will be on oral communication via power point presentations.

title: Seminar Imaging Sciences II
description: Seminar on new and emerging technologies in imaging sciences.

title: Elective Clinical Practicum I
description: Elective clinical practicum in radiography or sonography.

title: Princ of Radiation Physics
description: Introduces student to the nature and characteristic of radiation production and the fundamentals of photon interactions with matter. Course covers principles associated with radiography, nuclear medicine and radiation oncology.

title: Image Acquisition
description: Introduces students to the factors that govern the image production process.

title: Radiographic Pathology
description: Introduces student to concepts related to disease and etiological considerations with emphasis on radiographic appearance of disease and impact on exposure factor selection.

title: Clinical Practice IV
description: Content is designed to provide the knowledge base necessary to perform standard imaging procedures. Clinical practice is sequential. Students will be introduced to special imaging modalities.

title: Seminar Imaging Science
description: Introduces student to current research in imaging science.

title: Radiobiology
description: This course focuses on the biological impact of ionizing radiation.

title: Rad Image Analysis
description: Content is designed to provide a basis for analyzing and critiquing radiographic images.

title: Image Acquisition II
description: Content is designed to impart an understanding of the components, principles and operation of digital imaging systems found in diagnostic radiography, including quality management and control.

title: Clinical Practice V
description: Content is designed to provide the knowledge base necessary to perform standard imaging procedures. Clinical practice is sequential. Students will be introduced to special imaging modalities.

title: Seminar Imaging Sciences III
description: Seminar on new and emerging technologies in imaging sciences.

title: Elective Clinical Practicum II
description: Elective clinical practicum in radiography or sonography.

title: Imaging Procedures III
description: Content is designed to provide the knowledge necessary for advanced diagnostic radiographic imaging procedures.

title: Radiation Safety
description: This course focuses on radiation safety including ionizing radiation dose limits for the occupational worker, patient and general public.

title: Seminar in Imaging Sci
description: Review seminar for the primary ARRT certification examination.

title: Adv Practice Medical Img
description: Core theory requirement for all advanced practice students focused on discussion of communication, human diversity, health care policy, legal issues and patient information management.

title: Adv Sectional Anatomy
description: Provides students enrolled in CT/MRI advanced practice track advanced knowledge of sectional anatomy.

title: CT Procedures
description: Focus on advanced patient care skills including ACLS, imaging procedures and equipment in computed tomography.

title: MRI Procedures
description: Focus on advanced patient care skills including ACLS, imaging procedures and equipment in computed tomography.

title: Cardiovascular Anatomy
description: Focus on advanced cardiovascular anatomy, physiology and pathophysiology including heart anatomy, coronary, systemic, pulmonary, peripheral and cerebral circulation.

title: Vascular Interventional Img
description: Focus is on advanced patient care skills including ACLS, procedures and equipment utilized in cardiovascular and vascular/interventional imaging.

title: Adv Clinical Practice
description: Students will arrange clinical experience in selected imaging modality to gain competency in clinical procedures required to sit for post-primary ARRT certification exams.

title: Research Medical Imaging
description: Capstone course. Research methods and information literacy.

title: Transcultural Healthcare
description: Multidisciplinary approach to transcultural healthcare. Course will utilize comparative ethnography and provide a theoretical framework for organizing and interpreting information about health.

title: Radiography Management I
description:
Provides instruction in management principles for radiography
departmental managers including JACHO and Nuclear Regulatory
Commission parameters.  Students will be prepared to sit for the 
certification exam offered by the AHRA.

title: Radiography Management II
description:
Continuation of MI 412 to provide radiographer with management principles and
preparation for the AHRA certification exam in radiography management.

title: Mammography
description: Introduction to medical imaging of the breast.  Focus is to prepare student for advanced certification exam in Mammography.

title: RIS and PACS Principles
description: Course content provides basic knowledge of digital storage systems, computer networking, radiology information systems (RIS), and picture archiving and communication systems (PACS).

title: Advanced Clinical Practice II
description: Students will arrange clinical experience in selected imaging modality to gain competency in clinical procedures required to sit for post-primary ARRT certification exams.

title: Adv Trauma and Surgical Rad
description: Advanced practice course in trauma and surgical radiography for imaging sciences.

title: Forensic Radiology
description: This course will focus on introducing forensic radiography techniques and theoretical models.

title: Geriatric and Pediatric Rad
description: This course will focus on advanced diagnositc medical imaging in the geriatric and pediatric population including mobile radiography.

title: Mammography II
description: Advanced medical imaging of the breast.

title: Adv Clinical Practice III
description: Elective advanced clinical practicum in radiography or sonography.

title: Advanced MRI Theory
description: Advanced magnetic resonance imaging equipment and procedures.

title: Point of Care Ultrasound
description: This course will introduce basic principles of point of care ultrasound for vascular and cardiac interventional radiography, and mammography.

title: Cardiovascular Imaging
description: This course will focus on diagnostic and interventional procedures of the cardiovascular system.

title: Seminar ARRT Exam Review II
description: This is a review course for the ARRT primary certification examination.

title: Sonography Registry Review II
description: This course is designed to prepare the sonography student for their second specialty exam through the ARDMS.

title: Breast Sonography
description: This course covers anatomy, positioning and scanning protocol for the breast as well as an introduction to ultrasound physics.

title: Pediatric Echo I
description: This course focuses on sonographic techniques in pediatric echocardiography.

title: Pediatric Echo II
description: This course is a continuation of MI 439 and focuses on sonographic techniques in pediatric echocardiography.

title: Adv Echocardiography I
description: This course focuses on advanced sonographic techniques in pediatric echocardiography and is designed for the credentialed echocardiographer training in the area of pediatric echo.

title: Adv Echocardiography II
description: This course is a continuation of MI 441 and focuses on advanced sonographic techniques in pediatric echocardiography and is desinged for the credentialed echocardiographer training in the area of pediatric echo.

title: Vascular Interventional II
description: This course will focus on vascular interventional imaging equipment, devices and procedures.

title: Independent Study
description: Course designed to allow student to pursue individual research in medical imaging.

title: Independent Study
description: Course designed to allow student to pursue individual research in medical imaging.

title: Independent Study
description: Course designed to allow student to pursue individual research in medical imaging.

title: Independent Study
description: Course designed to allow student to pursue individual research in medical imaging.

title: Foundations of Officership
description: Increase self-confidence through team study and activities in physical fitness, land navigation, first aid, and basic drill.  Learn fundamental concepts of leadership in both classroom and outdoor laboratory environments. (CR: MS 101l)

title: Mil Sci Basic Lead Lab I
description: Learn and practice basic soldier skills and field craft. Build self-confidence and team building ledership skills through activities in drill, rappelling and basic marksmanship that can be applied throughout life.

title: Basic Leadership
description: Learn/apply principles of effective leading.  Develop communication skills to improve individual performance and group interaction.  Relate organizational ethical values to effectiveness of a leader.

title: Mil Sci Basic Lead Lab I
description: Learn and practice basic soldier skills and field craft. Build self-confidence and team-building leadership skills through activities in drill, rappelling and basic marksmanship that can be applied throughout life.

title: Individual Leadership Studies
description: Learn ethics-based leadership skills that develop individual abilities and contribute to team builidng.  Develop skills in communications, event planning, group coordination, advanced first aid navigation and basic tactics.

title: Mil Sci Basic Lead Lab II
description: Practice and refine the basic soldier skills and field craft learned in MS 101L /102L.  Build self-confidence and teambuilding skills through leadership opportunities supervising MS101L/MS102L cadets,

title: Leadership and Teamwork
description: Introduction to individual and team military tatics in squad operations.  Includes radio communications, safety assessments, movement techniques, planning for safety/ security, training management, and methods of preoperational checks.

title: Mil Sci Basic Lead Lab II
description: Practice and refine the basic soldier skills and field craft learned in MS101L/102L,  Build self confidence and teambuilding skills through leadership opportunities supervising MS101L/102L cadets, learned in MS101L/102L.  Build self-confidence and team building skills through leadership opportunities supervising MS101L/MS102L cadets.

title: Army Phys Readiness Prog
description: A physical training program consisting of Army conditioning drills, guerrilla exercises, and grass drills. (repeatable up to 4 hours)

title: Military History
description: Review the evolution of warfare, military theory and the military profession, with particular emphasis on the place of military institutions in society, as to develop a sense of historical awareness.

title: Leadership and Prob Solving
description: Uses small unit defensive tactics and opportunities to plan and conduct training.  Practical opportunities to lead small groups, receive personal assessments/encouragement, and lead in situations of increasing complexity.

title: Adv Course Lead Lab III
description: Students develop, practice and refine leadership skills by serving and being evaluated in a variety of leadership positions.  Students are responsible for the planning, coordination, execution and evaluation of training.

title: Leadership and Ethics
description: Analyze tasks; prepare written/oral guidance delegate, and supervise.  Plan for and adapt to the unexpected under stress.  Examine and apply lessons from leadership case studies.

title: Adv Course Lead Lab III
description: Students develop practice and refine leadership skills by serving and being evaluated in a variety of leadership positions.  Students are responsible for the planning, coordination, execution and evaluation of training.

title: Summer Training Camp
description: A five-week camp at an active arem post; it is highly structured and demanding, stressing leadership at small unit levels under varing, challenging conditions.  Individual leadership eveluated throughout camp.  (PR: MS 302 )

title: Leadership and Management
description: Articulate goals, plan, conduct, and evaluate activities of the ROTC cadet organization.  Assess organizational cohesion and develop improvement strategies.  Develop confidence in leadership skills and manage resources.

title: Adv Course Lead Lab IV
description: Students develop, practice and refine leadership skills by serving a variety of leadership positions.  Students are responsible for the planning, coordination, execution and evaluation of training for students enrolled in MS 101L - MS 302L .

title: Officership
description: Identify/resolve ethical dilemmas.  Refine counseling/ motivating techniques.  Examine aspects of tadition and law as relating to an Army officer.  Prepare for a future as a successful Army lieutenant.

title: Adv Course Lead Lab IV
description: Students develop, practice and refine leadership skills by serving a variety of leadership positions.  Students are responsible for the planning, coordination, execution and evaluation of training for students enrolled in MS 101L - MS 302L .

title: Mining Indust Hygiene Sci
description: Algebra, chemistry, human anatomy and physiology as applicable to Industrial Hygiene calculations; hazards encountered and physiological systems affected.

title: Survey of Mining
description: An overview of mining to provide the participant with a general understanding of mining history, development systems terminology, procedures, methods, and safety and health activities.

title: Mine Safety Prog Analysis
description: This course prepares the participant for the effective analysis of safety programs and provides some specific applications in the mining environment.
