title: Capstone Experience
description: Engages students in applying political science theories, methodologies, and techniques to construct evidence-based arguments. Focuses on analyzing political phenomena, synthesizing knowledge, and communicating insights effectively through rigorous research and presentation.

title: Psychology as a Major
description: Prepares students to be successful undergraduate Psychology majors and introduces possible careers and educational requirements.

title: Introductory Psychology (CT)
description: Principles and methods in the scientific study of behavior.

title: General Psychology-Honors (CT)
description: For the superior student.

title: Psychology of Adjustment
description: Modes of personal and social adjustment; assessment and treatment techniques.

title: Intro Paraprof Mental Health
description:
Course covers paraprofessional mental health career options; community
resource utilization, deinstitutionalization, crisis/interpersonal intervention and special populations.  On-site observation experience required.

title: Elem Behavioral Stat
description: Orientation to the philosophy of science; survey of methods in behavior study; elementary statistics.

title: Elem Behavioral Stats Lab
description: A laboratory course accompanying PSY 223 , emphasizing experiential excercises related to methodology and research in psychology.

title: Psychology of Pop Culture
description: This course surveys how psychology and psychological issues are presented, researched, and applied in multiple modalities of popular culture.

title: Paranormal Phenomena
description: Investigation of such putative paranormal events as ESP, clairvoyance, UFO's, ghosts, astral projecttion, astrology, and related topics.  Emphasis on evaluation of evidence using the scientific method and scientific criteria.

title: Social Psychology
description: Social determinants of individual behavior.

title: Beh Int Paraprof Mental Health
description: Course covers behavioral theory, antecedent motivators, functional analysis, reinforcement regimes; preparing and following treatment plans, defining measurable goals/objectives, monitoring progress.

title: Child Development
description: Psychological characteristics and personal and social problems of developmental periods.

title: Adult Development
description: Study of the physiological, psychological, and social processes that occur throughout adult development.

title: Experimental Psychology
description: Methodology and research in psychology.  2 lec-2 lab.

title: Sensation & Perception
description: Methodology and research in sensory and perceptual processes.

title: Human Sexual Behavior
description: A psychological approach to the functioning, attitudes, varieties and development of human sexual behavior.

title: Animal Behavior
description: A comprehensive study of the behavior of non-human animals.

title: Personality
description: Personality structure, dynamics and development.

title: Psychology Of Aggression
description: A multifaceted study of aggressive behavior in humans and other animals.  Topics include biological and learned components of aggression, sex differences in aggression, violent crimes and personalities, media violence, and control of personal and group aggesssion.

title: Sport Psychology
description: An examination of theory, research, and application of psychological principles related to sport and exercise.

title: Psychometrics
description: Mental test theory and applicatons.

title: Abnormal Psychology
description: An overview of the theories, assessment techniques, and treatment of maladaptive behavior.

title: Adv Topics Developmental Psy
description: This course will provide an advanced study of topics regarding change throughout the lifespan.   Emphasis will be on understanding the interactions of biological, psychological and social factors.

title: Cognitive Psychology
description: Theoretical and empirical approaches to the role of pattern recognition, attention, memory, language, problem solving and decision making in human thinking.

title: Psychology of Learning
description: Critical study of the major theories of learning and the related research.

title: Inter Behavioral Stat
description: An intermediate level presentation of descriptive and inferential statistics as applied in behavioral research. 2 lec-2 lab.

title: Psychology of Personnel
description: Psychological principles and methods applied to functions in personnel administration.

title: Intro to I-O Psychology
description: A systematic study of the application of psychological methods and principles in business and industry.  Emphasis is on research methods, motivation, training, leadership, personnel selection, employee safety, and job satisfaction.

title: Psychology of Cinema Symbolism
description: Analysis of how signs, symbols, and archetypes influence the individual and collective psyche via film.

title: Cross Cultural Psychology
description: Emic and etic cultural concepts are considered from an American (subcultural) and international perspective. Cultural influences on healing, health and service are covered.

title: Psychology of Women and Gender
description: This course explores theories, findings, and social issues regarding the psychology of women and gender, with emphasis on gender role socialization on people's beliefs and behaviors across the lifespan.

title: Currt Models Psychotherapy
description: Introduction of theoretical models and related therapeutic strategies which influence the practice of modern psychotherapy.

title: Psych of Conspiracy Theory
description: Understand, identify, and give critical analysis to the psychological and social factors that promote the support and belief of conspiracy theory.

title: Physiological Psychology
description: The relationships between physiological functions and biochemical processes and behavior.

title: Health Psychology
description: Introduction to the contribution of psychology to the promotion and maintenance of health and the prevention and treatment of illness.

title: Evolutionary Psychology
description: Wide-ranging examination of the implications of the theory of evolution for understanding human behavior.

title: Research in Psychology
description: Laboratory courses to give advanced students experience in conducting psychological research.

title: Research in Psychology

title: History & Systems
description: An examination of the historical and philosophical antecedents of contemporary psychology.

title: Love Intimacy and Attachment
description: Examination of how childhood attachments, bonds, and relationships affect and influence adult perspectives on love, expectations, intimacy, fidelity, and commitment.

title: I-O Psych Practicum
description: The course will offer students applied observational/research experience in Personnel/Human Resource Departments under the supervision of professionals within the fields of Industrial-Organizational Psychology and Human Resources. (PR: Psy 4l8 or Psy 420 and Permission.)

title: Practicum in Clinical PSY
description: Students work 6 hours per week in a local clinical setting where they have the opportunity to observe individual and group therapy, psychological testing, staff meetings, etc. PR: l2 hours of PSY including 408 and Permission of Instructor

title: Race Culture and Development
description: Examine the roles that race, ethnicity, and culture play in the physical, cognitive, intellectual, and social developmental processes of people of color.

description: Examination of infectious diseases from a public health perspective, including strategies for prevention, treatment, control and eradication.

title: Community Health & Devpt

title: Global Health (CT)
description: This course provides students the opportunity to study healthcare systems in developed and developing countries and compare these systems to the US healthcare systems.

description: This course develops conceptual and methodological skills for design and implementation of Public Health policy. This philosophy of scientific inquiry and systems approaches to problem solving are covered.

description: This course provides an overview of how information and knowledge are acquired and applied to public health problems. The philosophy of scientific inquiry and systems approaches to problem solving are covered.

description: Introduction to policy development, establishment and implementation with a focus on critical health issues.  Students will develop skills in addressing current problems in health policy.

description: In this course, students will be introduced to health policy and the process by which it is developed. Various approaches to the health policy are defined and their rationale considered.

title: Topics in Health Policy
description: This course provides in depth study of timely topics in health policy.  High impact health reform issues will be examined, culminating in student formulation of a policy proposal.

title: Monitoring and Eval in PH
description: Introduces students to the language and theory of program monitoring and evaluation.  Facilitate understanding of managing and tracking results in public health programs.

title: Urban and Rural Health
description: Explores Public Health issues associated with rapid growth of urban population in developing countries and United States of America.

title: PH Competencies (Capstone)
description: All students are expected to master a set of competencies during the course of their studies. The competencies that have been selected by the faculty of the College of Health Professions are derived from the Ten Essential Public Health Services.

description: The course focuses on theoretical aspects of quantitative research methods in public health and health care.

title: Quant Research Methods Appl
description: This course focuses on applications and techniques of quantitative research methods.

title: Comm/Non Comm Disease

description: A comparative study of major religious traditions of the Western world: Judaism, Christianity, Islam, Zorastrianism and religions of the Americas.

title: Religious Traditions Asia
description: A comparative study of the major religious traditions of Asia: Hinduism, Buddhism, Confucianism, Taoism and Shinto, and how these religions have influenced Asian thought and cultures.

title: Biblical Geography
description: Students explore the physical and human geography of ancient Palestine and assess how geography informs our understanding of people, events, and places described by Jewish and Christian scriptures.

title: Lit of Old Testament
description: Traces the origins, growth and development of the literature of Hebrew people to the Greek period. Includes an introduction to and application of modern tools of biblical study.

title: Lit of New Testament
description: Traces the origins, growth, and development of the literature of the early Christian church. Includes an introduction to and application of modern tools of biblical study.

description: An interdisciplinary course to introduce students to the elements of a humanistic education through study of film.  (Same as Philosophy 250 and Classics 250; PR or CR: ENG 101 )

description: Treats the cultures and religions of Egypt from the Neolithic to Late Periods, and their impacts and receptions in ancient and modern cultures.  Archaeology art literature and history based.

description: An analysis of the nature of religious personalities, institutions, literature, philosophies, experiences, and education.

title: World of Islam
description: An examination of the global cultures of Islam with particular focus upon the origin and development of the religion which binds them together.

title: The Teachings of Jesus
description: An analysis of early Christian writing and a systematic study of the message of the historical Jesus that stands behind it.

title: Early Christianity
description: Traces the background, birth, and development of Christian thought from Paul through Augustine.

title: The Hebrew Prophets
description: The rise of the office of prophet and the contributions of prophecy to religion.

title: Jewish Holocaust
description: An examination of the religious/philosophical questions raised by the Holocaust of European Jews:  Why and how did it occur?  What does it tell us about religion and humanity?

title: ABCs of Orthodoxy
description: An examination of Eastern Orthodox Christianity from the culture in which it was birthed to its place in today's society.

title: The Protestant Faith
description: An examination of the distinctive historical and theological features of the Protestant movement in Western Christendom, with special attention to the distinctive beliefs and practices of contemporary American denominations.

title: Religion in America
description: The rise and development of religious thinking in America. (Same as History 323)

title: The Jewish Way of Life
description: An exploration of the distinctive features of the heritage of modern Judaism.  An integrated approach to the study of Jewish religious practices, teachings, literature, and contributions to contemporary life.

title: Classics of Rel Lit
description: A contextual analysis of selected popular religious classics, e.g., Foxe's Book of Martyrs, Bunyan's Pilgrim's Progress, St. Augustine's Confessions, Bhagavad-Gita, and the like.

title: Hindu Mysticism
description: A general survey of religious life and mysticism throughout South Asian history.

title: Buddhism
description: General survey of Buddhist life throughout history and around the world.

title: Respiratory Pharmacology
description: Introduces the student to basic pharmacology of medicines used in respiratory care and physiological implications on the human body.

title: Intro to Respiratory Care
description: Introduces the student to the history of respiratory care and professional organizations. Emphasis is on the role of the respiratory therapist as a member of the health care team.

title: Intro Resp Care Procedures
description: Administration of medical gases, humidity and aerosol therapy with emphasis on the handling of medical gases and safety in administration. Techniques of therapeutic procedures used in respiratory care are included.

title: Respiratory Lab
description: Administration of medical gases, humidity and aerosol therapy with emphasis on the handling of medical gases and safety in administration. Techniques of therapeutic procedures used in respiratory care are included.

title: Concepts of Respiratory Care
description: Designed for the board certified and state licensed CRT to enter into advanced respiratory practice. Emphasis is on concepts and principles for professional practice as an RRT.

title: Pulmonary Pathophys
description: Emphasis is placed on the etiology, signs and symptoms, pathology, clinical manifestations, sequellae, and treatment. The respiratory therapist's role in the recognition and treatment of pulmonary diseases is highlighted.

title: Mech Vent Tech & MGT
description: An introduction to the fundamentals of mechanical ventilation techniques and terminology. Monitoring and the ability to solve clinical problems relating to mechanical ventilation are emphasized. Lab included.

title: Respiratory Internship I
description: Emphasis is on information gathering from the patient record, physical evaluation of the patient and basic respiratory interventions.

title: Pulmonary Rehab/Home Care
description: Emphasis on the care of the patient with long term pulmonary disability requiring home care. Psychosocial and physical needs are addressed with emphasis of quality of life and  cardiopulmonary reserve.

title: Cardiopulmonary Diag
description: Emphasis on advanced techniques of pulmonary function testing. Topics include lung volume determination, diffusion/distribution of ventilation, invasive and non invasive methods of arterial blood gas sampling, analysis, and interpretation.

title: Neonatal/Pediatric Care
description: Provide knowledge of neonatal/pediatric patients; fetal cardiopulmonary development and changes at birth, care methods used and evaluation of neonatal and pediatric patients are covered.

title: Intro Critical Care MGT
description: Designed to provide the student with knowledge of airway management, transtrachael oxygen therapy and aspiration, broncholscopy, thoracentisis, pleural chest tubes, arterial lines, ABG interpretation and analysis, transports, and electrocardiogram interpretation.

title: Respiratory Care Seminar
description: Introduces the student to NBRC exam taking skills, mock examinations of the NBRC matrix, realistic clinical problems and situations with emphasis on critical thinking and problem solving.

title: Respiratory Internship II
description: Emphasis is on supervised practice of humidity and aerosol therapy, aerosol drug therapy, lung inflation therapy and techniques used in electrocardiography.

title: Respiratory Internship III
description: Emphasis is on supervised practice of arterial blood gas sampling and analysis, arterial line management, chest tube management, ECGs observation of hemodynamic measurement and monitoring, IABP management.

title: Dynamics of Pulmonary
description: Emphasis is placed on the interaction of systems in gas exchange and renal involvement in acid base balance.

title: Acute/Chronic Pulmonary Mgt
description: This is a 3 hour couse with emphasis placed on pulmonary function testing/interpretation and care of a patient with long term pulmonary disability requiring home care and rehabilitation.

title: Intro Resp Care MGT
description: Introduces the student to the basic principles of management in the respiratory care department. Includes theory, scope of management, quality issues, budgeting, personnel issues, evaluation and application of management concept.

title: Respiratory Internship IV
description: Emphasis is on cardiopulmonary assessment and treatment of trauma, post-surgical, cardiac, renal, neonatal and pediatric patients with refinement of monitoring procedures and interpretation of data.

title: Clinical Respiratory Education
description: Designed as an introduction to clinical teaching in a respiratory care program. Emphasis is on instructional and evaluation strategies and development of performance objectives.

title: Adv Neonatal & Pediatrics
description: Advanced study of neonatal/pediatric pathophysiology including parenchymal disease, obstructive airway disease, lesions of the lungs and airways, congenital abnormalities, respiratory distress syndrome, apnea disorders, neurological disorders and trauma. (PR: RSP 206 )

title: Resp Cost MGT & Solutions
description: Introduces the student to cost solutions for respiratory departments. Topics include annual budgets, purchasing decisions, effective staffing, inventory and supply controls and cost-containment methods.

title: Resp Care Performance Improv
description: Provides basic principles associated with Total Quality Management (TQM) and Continuous Quality Improvement (CQI) to aid in problem identification and quality problem-solving for respiratory care departments.

title: Adv Tech Adult Critical Care
description: Emphasis is on current respiratory care procedures for the critically ill adult patient with exploration into newer techniques.

title: Resp Mgt & Quality Improvement
description: This course introduces the student to basic management principles of a respiratory department.  Discussion includes, scope of management, quality issues, budgeting, personnel issues, and evaluation and application of management concepts.

title: Adv Mechanical Ventilation
description: Advanced theory and application of mechanical ventilation.  Study of various mechanical ventilators and artificial airways, with major emphasis placed on all aspects of the management of the patient-ventilator system.

title: Intro to Sleep Disorders
description: Designed to teach how a polysomnogram is performed, the major categories of sleep disorders, the presenting symptoms of sleep apnea, narcolepsy, psycholphysiological insomnia and sleep disturbance due to depression.

title: Respiratory MGT
description: Designed to examine respiratory care in rual America. This course will address the key issues confronting rural  respiratory healthcare today, examine the causes and develop solutions to the issues.

title: Respiratory Care Research
description: Designed to provide the student knowledge about survey of research problems, methods, and designs utilized in respiratory care, with emphasis on data presentation and analysis.

title: Adv Respiratory Care Practicum
description: Advanced respiratory techniques and management for clients across the life-span.

title: Flight/Hyperbaric Care
description: Advanced respiratory techniques related to physiologic stressors impacting patient care due to atmospheric impact and pressure gradients and unique hazards in these environments to patients and staff.

title: Community Respiratory Care
description: Designed for the student to provide care in a variety of settings including clinics, schools and other settings utilizing principles of public health and client and family teaching.

title: Clinical Decision Making
description: The course focuses on gathering appropriate clinical information to make evidence-based decisions in the treatment for respiratory care diseases.

title: Capstone in Respiratory Care
description: Role synthesis practicum incorporating provider of care, coordinator of care, member of profession and leadership roles.

description: Introduction to occupational safety and health on an international level.  Comparisons of various accidents by type and country will be explored along with prevention techniques.

title: Safety Technology Co-Op
description: A supervised experience in which the student works for a company to gain practical experience in a students major.

title: Prin Ergonomics & Hum Factors
description: Introductory principles within human-machine relationships; examining the biological, physiological, and psychological factors which contribute to accident causation.

title: Prin of Ergonomics Lab
description: A laboratory course designed to include the principles and applications of human factors/ergonomics that were introduced in SFT 373 .

title: Traffic Safety & Driver Educa
description: An introductory course in the teaching of safety and driver education, including techniques of classroom and behind-the- wheel instruction. 2 lec-2 lab.

title: Traffic Law Enforcement
description: A course designed to study and evaluate the varied and complex system of laws governing the control of all forms of traffic and the influences and responsibilities of traffic law enforcement in presentday society.

title: International Safety & Health
description: The course covers the impact of globalization on worldwide safety and health programs, and a wide variety of safety and health programs for various countries and multinational organizations.

title: Industrial Hygiene
description: Environmental protection as related to industrial settings. Air/water quality, noise and chemical pollution and hazardous material control.

title: Industrial Hygiene Lab
description: Quantitative monitoring techniques for measuring air and water quality, the measurement of noise and chemical pollutions and the evaluation of other physical hazards.

title: Hospital Safety
description: The course covers the various aspects of safety and health in professional health care services.

title: Safety Training Methods

title: Regression Analysis

title: Experimental Designs

title: Sampling Designs & Estimation
description: Coverage of the theory and applications of a variety of sampling designs; sample size determination; ratio and regression estimates; comparisions among the designs.

title: Stat Methods and Applications
description: Aspects of statistical modeling including model building, adequacy assessment, inference, and prediction. Applications to social, biological, health and medical sciences, engineering, and industry.

title: Probability & Statistics II

title: Statistical Computing
description: Introduction to the commonly used statistical computing techniques, procedures and methods, with extensive use of R language and environment, and SAS for statistical computing and graphics.

title: Applied Survival Analysis

title: Independent Study
description: A faculty, supervised, indvidualized course of study of a topic in statistics.

description: Students enrolled in this course learn the terminology, techniques, and principles required to execute lighting designs for theatre, dance and film through "hands-on" experiences and projects.

title: Intro to Technical Theatre
description: Technical production, scenic construction and stage operations are explored including critical application of traditional and current computer technologies within the profession.  Lecture and supervised laboratory application links theory with practice.

title: Introduction to Costuming
description: This introductory course exposes students to the elementary principles, techniques, terminology, materials and application used to realize theatrical costumes.  A combination of lecture and laboratory instruction links theory with practice.

title: Special Topics
description: Program of study not normally covered in other courses. Topics vary from semester to semester.

title: Music Theatre Workshop I
description: Applied skills in musical theatre for chorus and ensemble roles, including song presentation and integration of musical and dramatic elements. Intended for beginning and intermediate students.

title: Theatre Management
description: Theories and practices in performing arts management.  An exploration of fiscal and physical management techniques, including budgeting, box office procedures, promotion, and staffing.

title: Acting II: Scene Study
description: Development of skill through exercises and analytical study of scenes. (PR: THE 222 and successful completion of Sophomore Review or permission of instructor.)

title: Stage Voice II: Dialects
description: Study and practice of dialects and accents that are commonly used in acting plays.

title: Stage Mvmt II: Phys Approaches
description: Advanced exploration of movement and its application to character development and text. (PR: THE 222 and successful completion of Sophomore Review or permission of instructor.)

title: Theatre Career Marketing
description: Examination of strategic marketing and personal branding techniques for designing, enhancing, and promoting a professional image for the theatre artist.

title: Drafting & Rendering
description: The application of drafting and rendering conventions utilized in the planning and execution of theatrical productions.  Mechanical drawing, computer assisted drawing, freehand sketching and color application techniques will be employed.

title: Stage Decor
description: Identification of artistic, historical , social, philosophical and technical influences that diverse cultures offer theatrical designers.  Research, critical examination, drawing, digital documentation culminate in the creation of industry standard electronic portfolios.

title: Stage Make-up
description: Development of character make-up designs through analysis, research and application of various make-up media suitable to stage, print and film.  Adherence to industry standards of hygiene, sanitation and professional etiquette.

title: Costume Design
description: Practical and psychological aspects of design.  Study of design theory, script analysis, rendering techniques, fabric choices.  Development of designs from initial concept to final renderings.

title: Costume Construction
description: A hands-on approach to the techniques of theatrical costuming.  Period method by machine and hand, industrial machine and some pattern making.  Work on classroom projects and University productions.

title: Scene Design I
description: Practical application of aesthetic and technical principles of scene design for the proscenium stage.  Script analysis, production concepts, architectural research, mechanical and AutoCAD drawings, white models, painter's elevations are utilized.

title: Scene Painting
description: Exploration of various techniques utilized by scenic artists including the generation of painter's evaluations, design renderings, estimating scenic demands, media selection and execution of designs in full scale.

title: Stage Management
description: The responsibilities and duties of stage management are explored to prepare students entering the profession. Principles and practices of stage management are applied through scheduling, budgeting, running, cueing and safety.

title: Theatre Practicum
description: Acting, directing, or technical work in Marshall University Theatre productions. Register only with permission of instructor. May be repeated for credit.

title: Music Theatre Workshop II
description: Applied skills in musical theatre including integration of dance, music, and dialogue in ensemble performance. Intended for students majoring in musical theatre. (repeatable for two semesters)

title: Acting Styles
description: Interpretation of roles from classical, romantic, neoclassical, and modern plays. (PR: THE 222 and successful completion of Sophomore Review or permission of instructor.)

title: Auditioning II: Prof. Aspects
description: Students develop skills and prepare materials for professional acting auditions.

title: Children's Theatre
description: Theory, direction and staging of plays for children.  PR: Permission of instructor or Theatre Program Director.  May be repeated for a total of 9 hours.

title: Directing I
description: Introduction to theories, principles, techniques, and history of directing.

title: Theatre History To 1660
description: Survey of man's activities in the theatre from primitive times to 1660.

title: Theatre Hist Since 1660
description: Survey of man's activities in the theatre from 1660 to present.

title: Stage Lighting II
description: Advanced study in lighting design principles utilized for non-proscenium stages and/or impressionistic productions will be emphasized.  Combined visual, manual and computer generated documentation will comprise portfolio for final critique.

title: Freshman First Class Honors

title: New Student Seminar
description: An in-depth introduction to college life, covering areas such as academic expectations and skills, personal adjustments, social issues.  Intended for freshmen.

title: Strat for Academic Success
description: An academic enrichment course which provides students with strategies and practical experience for academic success. Topics to be covered include research skills, critical thinking applications, and effective study skills.

title: Career Dev Undergrad Students
description: Designed for undergraduate college students for career and major exploration. Topics include interest testing, career information, decision-making skills, and job-finding strategies. Course may count as an elective toward graduation.

title: Adv Strat for Acad Success
description: Course provides practical strategies for assessing personal and academic challenges, honing critical thinking skills to make informed decisions, improving learning and time management skills, tracking progress toward personal goals.

title: Peer Mentoring
description: Students trained as peer advisors will lead discussions, campus field trips and work with faculty advisors in the design and implementation of the freshman introduction to campus life and classes. (Prerequisite: Permission of the Executive Director CAE or Dean of University College)

title: Introduction to Scholarship
description: Prepares students to participate on an active research team, with the guidance of (a) faculty research mentor(s), pursing a line of research.

title: Scholarship Discovery
description: Engages students in original research that advances knowledge and seeks to improve community conditions.

title: Strategy Successful Career Dev
description: Students will learn tools to navigate the workforce successfully by exploring, identifying, and preparing for future career paths; and developing job search skills in today's advanced technological climate.

title: Graduate School Preparation

title: Develop PE & Sport in US
description: A survey of the development of sport forms and physical education curricula from colonial America through the present day.

title: HE & PE in Elementary Schools
description: Provides students with a wide array of knowledge and skills so they can effectively assume teacher responsibilities in elementary health and physical education programs.

title: Teaching Individual Sports
description: Study and application of the principles and techniques of teaching individual sports skills in grades 5-12 (PR: Completion of Physical Education Activity Competencies).

title: Teaching Team Sports
description: Study and application of the principles and techniques of teaching team sports skills in grades 5-12 (PR: Completion of Physical Education Activity Competencies).

title: Phys Ed Elementary School
description: A practical approach designed to aid the elementary teacher in teaching methods and techniques needed for the teaching of elementary physical education.  (PR:  Majors, WELL 350 )

title: School & Comm Health
description: An examination of some of the specific relationships between school and community health programs, including the roles and interaction of public, professional, private and voluntary health agencies with the school.  (PR: HS 220 , 221 , 321 )

title: Rhy & Movement Children
description: Provides elementary physical education specialists with an overview of rhythms and movement activities for elementary school children.

title: Curriculum in Health Ed
description: A study of principles, objectives, and procedures in curriculum development for middle and secondary school programs including historical and philosophical perspectives and comparing current curricula. (PR: HS 220 , 221 , 321 , 325)

title: Adapted PE & Mainstream
description: Theory of remedial exercise and individualizing of physical activities to meet the needs of the physically handicapped.  (PR HS 201 )

title: Curriculum Development PE
description: A study of principles, objectives and procedures in curriculum construction in the elementary and secondary school programs.  Typical programs studied and evaluated.  (PR: Completion of Physical Education activity competencies)

title: Intro Women Gender Sexuality
description: An interdisciplinary introduction to women's, gender, and sexuality studies drawing material from the humanities, social sciences, and natural sciences.

title: Advanced Topics in WGS
description: An upper-level, interdisciplinary exploration of women's, gender, and sexuality studies drawing material from the humanities, social sciences, and natural sciences.
